findings.title: iPad layout = bottom-right course with dead upper-left void
findings.note: The screenshot-based void was on the OLD left->right >1.6x-viewport course with installCameraFollow. Current code replaces it with a CONFINED VERTICAL ZIG-ZAG COLUMN centered on size.width/2 (±composedColOffset=105), drops camera-follow entirely, and tiers climb playableGroundY->composedCeilingY filling the full height. The whole climb fits one resting frame. Recommendation: none — fix landed.

findings.title: Finale left landing lane only ~10pt, contradicting the ~50pt promise
findings.note: Stale geometry. Reviewers cite span sweeping back to finaleLeft+10. Current spec0 = (finaleLeft+70, span +60) sweeps RIGHT to +130, so the leftmost spike edge is 70-12=58pt from the rung's left edge — the ~50pt clear lane IS honored (58pt > Bit half-width 11). No fix needed.

findings.title: calmOverlay!/exitBlocker! force-unwraps can crash
findings.note: Both are assigned on the immediately preceding lines (635-640, 708-712) and unwrapped in the same scope before any path can nil them. Never nil at the unwrap site. Not a crash.

findings.title: HUD parented to camera before installCameraFollow exists -> nil gameCamera
findings.note: Moot: current code DROPPED camera-follow on every path. moonIcon, calmOverlay, and the DND button are all scene-fixed (addChild(self)), no camera parenting. The cited bug cannot occur.

findings.title: notePlayerStruggle() never overridden -> only generic 18-22s fallback hint
findings.note: It does not need overriding. handleDeath() calls notePlayerStruggle(), which escalates the level's own hintText() (the explicit Control-Center crescent-moon string + CAN'T DO THIS? fallback) at struggleCount>=2 after 8s — FASTER than the 22s wall-clock net. Plus presentLockedExitCue() fires on door contact. Hint escalation is fully wired.

findings.title: Focus activation does not call notePlayerProgress() -> hint timer not reset at the key realization
findings.note: Minor: enabling Focus is the puzzle's solve moment but doesn't reset the no-progress/struggle timer, so a hint could still fire moments after the player already figured it out. Harmless (the door also opens, ending the struggle). One-liner: call notePlayerProgress() inside updateFocusState when enabled becomes true. Low priority.

findings.title: Visible 'CAN'T DO THIS?' fallback button spoils that this is a toggle, not a system-state puzzle
findings.note: Legit design tension: the always-present fallback (plus the two bare moon HUD dots gemini/claude found ambiguous) tips the hand before the player discovers the real iOS Focus mechanic. Recommendation: gate the CAN'T DO THIS? button behind first struggle (reveal after a death or ~15s), matching the earned-hint cadence the level already uses elsewhere. Note it is the deliberate anti-softlock affordance, so keep it — just delay it.

findings.title: iPad interior climb is a chain of max-budget (130pt H + ~one-tier V) diagonal hops with one-fall-to-floor reset
findings.note: Every interior rung strictly alternates ±composedColOffset, so consecutive rungs are 210pt center-to-center; with width-80 rungs the edge-to-edge gap is exactly 130 (== hard cap maxJumpableGap) AND simultaneously a ~one-tier rise, i.e. the hardest combined-vector jump in the game, repeated up the whole column, with hazards bobbing over each rung and a full reset to tier-0 on any miss. Within budget (not a softlock) but punishing for a teaching level. Recommendation: widen the alternating rungs to 90-100 (drops gap to ~110-120) or reduce composedColOffset to ~95 to ease the diagonal.

findings.title: Frozen finale patrols could wall off the door
findings.note: Investigated and cleared: when Focus latches the gate open the two patrols are isPaused at arbitrary mid-sweep X, but two 24pt-wide spikes cannot cover the ~104pt finale span continuously, and Bit can hop a single frozen spike to reach the exit body [+120,+160]. Always completable. Not a bug — recording the analysis so it isn't re-raised.

topActions: Delay the 'CAN'T DO THIS?' fallback button (and consolidate/label the bare moon HUD dots) until first struggle so the real iOS Focus mechanic stays a discovery — keep it as the anti-softlock escape, just gate it (polish).

Ease the iPad interior climb: widen alternating rungs to ~95-100pt or drop composedColOffset to ~95 so each hop isn't simultaneously the 130pt-horizontal AND one-tier-vertical max-budget diagonal (polish/fairness).
Call notePlayerProgress() in updateFocusState when Focus turns on, so the hint timer resets at the solve moment (cosmetic one-liner).

verdictLine: ship — 1 real fairness-polish survivor (t=0 POWER-button discoverability, already safety-netted by hints), 5 false-positives, 0 crashes/softlocks; the physics gate and both layout paths are verified correct

findings.title: No t=0 teaching of the POWER-button -> gravity link (bidirectional toggle undertaught on first encounter)
findings.note: Genuinely present: the t=0 panel ('THE BATTERY IS DYING / EVERYTHING GETS LIGHTER WHEN I LET GO') is atmospheric and never links the bottom-right POWER button to gravity, and the hardest idea (OFF for the narrow drop, ON for the chasm) is untaught until the struggle-gated hint. NOT a softlock: notePlayerStruggle() fires per death (line 871), notePlayerProgress() resets on first low-power engage (line 766), and the BaseLevelScene 22s wall-clock fallback (BaseLevelScene.swift:714-718) both surface hintText(), which names the button location and both toggle directions precisely. Recommendation: add a one-time diegetic cue near the POWER button (or on the chasm's first death) so the button->gravity link is discoverable a beat earlier without spoiling the reversal; low priority since the escalation already prevents a stuck state.

findings.title: Always-visible manual POWER toggle 'undercuts the device-feature premise'; gate it behind isLowPowerModeEnabled availability
findings.note: Wrong/harmful as stated. Real Low Power Mode IS wired: PowerModeManager observes NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange and posts .lowPowerModeChanged, which routes through handleGameInput -> setLowPower (the same single source of truth as the button), so the device feature is functional. The button is deliberately additive. DeepSeek's proposed gate (hide unless real LPM is undetectable) would SOFTLOCK the level: the puzzle is bidirectional (needs OFF for the drop, ON for the chasm), which would force the player to round-trip iOS Settings twice mid-level and breaks entirely in the simulator. Leave the button always-visible. No change.

findings.title: degradePlatformVisuals permanently mutates alpha/lineWidth and stacks / fails to restore on repeated toggles
findings.note: Attributed to Gemini in round 1; verified false. The normal-gravity branch unconditionally resets surface.path to a fresh solid rect, alpha to 1.0, and lineWidth to the lineWidth constant on every platform regardless of prior state. It restores from constants, not from a saved snapshot, so it is fully idempotent — toggling low<->normal any number of times cannot stack or drift. No saved-original-values fix is needed.

findings.title: Finale depends on a 620 dy launch but BitCharacter applies only a 470 impulse; apex *150 divisor unvalidated
findings.note: Verified false. The 470 impulse produces a velocity change of ~8836 pt/s on Bit's body mass, so jump() ALWAYS clamps to exactly maxJumpVelocity=620 (line 851-854) — the launch is reliably 620 every time. The *150 is the pt<->physics-unit scale (gravity dy=-14 = -2100 pt/s^2); apex=620^2/(2*14*150)=91.52 at normal g, which exactly matches the engine's own BaseLevelScene.absoluteMaxRise=91, and 256.27 at low g. Confirmed: catch-top 140 -> normal peak body-bottom 231.5 (below shelf 360, undershoots = mechanic) and low peak 396.3 (above 360, clears with 36pt margin). Gate is correct on iPhone and iPad.

findings.title: iPad composed climb's same-tier beats overlap into a confusing flat run
findings.note: Verified false. Consecutive same-tier beats (the FLAT REST [3,3] and the dip+return tails) sit at the SAME Y but on OPPOSITE column sides: X is authored as center +/- colOffset alternating by index parity (line 397), so two consecutive beats are 2*colOffset=128pt apart center-to-center. With the capped widths (REST<=118), edge-to-edge clearance is ~27-37pt — positive, never overlapping. The zig-zag rhythm holds; no overlap bug.

findings.title: Force-unwrapped IUOs (bit, playerController, batteryIndicator) risk a crash
findings.note: Not a real crash path here. contactDelegate is set in configureScene before buildLevel, but every body created before setupBit (platforms = static ground, death zone = static hazard) is static; static-static pairs generate no contacts, so didBegin/didEnd cannot fire before bit exists. touchesBegan calls playerController only after setupBit ran in the same synchronous configureScene. The IUOs are conventional SpriteKit setup-order unwraps, not a latent trap.

findings.title: Grounded state tracked with naive begin/end (no contact counter) — multi-contact desync
findings.note: L15 does NOT adopt the BaseLevelScene sharedGroundPlatform contact-counter pattern; it sets grounded=true on any ground begin and grounded=false 0.05s after any ground end. If the player ever contacted two ground bodies at once, leaving one would clear grounded while still standing on the other. In practice harmless on this level: platforms are well-separated (iPad zig-zag) or vertically stacked but spatially distinct (iPhone), the 0.05s delay buffers single-frame flicker, and PlayerController's 0.16s coyote window covers any transient. No player-facing impact; flagging only as a latent-pattern note, not worth changing.

findings.title: iPad spawn comment says '~35pt clear' but code uses +50; dead composedCourseExtent=0 scaffolding
findings.note: Doc/code mismatch: setupBit spawns the iPad player at composedSpawn.y+50 (body-bottom ~23pt above tier-0 top — safe, just a slightly longer settle) while the comment claims it matches the ~35pt phone margin. Separately, composedCourseExtent is hardcoded 0 so the installCameraFollow guard at line 722 is permanently dead. Both are intentional/inert (the guard is retained as a documented future opt-in) and player-invisible; clean up the comment if touching the file, otherwise leave.

topActions: Add a single one-time diegetic cue tying the POWER button to gravity on first encounter (e.g. a brief pulse/label near the button or a gravity-arrow flash on the chasm's first death), so the bidirectional toggle is discoverable one beat before the struggle-gated hint — the only real survivor, and low priority since the hint escalation already prevents a stuck state.

verdictLine: Solid, completability-hardened level: the forced rotten-platform trap makes shake-to-undo genuinely load-bearing, the free trap-repair undo + undoCount floor + inert-exit gate close every softlock, and deltaTime is base-clamped so no resume/history edge bites. No crash or softlock survives. The only real residual items are minor polish (a redundant competing .move on the moving platform, children.first surface capture, and a stale-against-old-build iPad dead-sky claim that is now ~60pt at worst).

findings.title: iPad composed climb leaves the top half a dead green void (route not lifted high enough)
findings.note: Stale-screenshot finding: claude reviewed the OLD shallow-diagonal build. The CURRENT confined-column build sizes tierCount via ipadFillTierCount (band/(N-1)<=85) and places PEAK/FINALE at tierY(topTier), which lands near ipadCeilingY=topSafeY-150. On a 1366-tall iPad the band is ~1082, step clamps to 85, topTier lands ~60pt below the ceiling — a small residual dead band, not an empty top half. The beat list (TEACH..while cur<topTier-1..PEAK/breath/finale) genuinely reaches topTier. Not the severe void described.

findings.title: repairTrap() does not consume an undo charge, breaking the limited-undo premise
findings.note: Intentional and load-bearing, documented at the performUndo SOFTLOCK GUARD comment. The forced trap is the ONLY route to the exit; if undoCount hit 0 on ordinary traversal undos the trap would be permanently unwinnable. So the trap-repair undo is free (undoCount floored to max(undoCount,1), no decrement when repairingTrap) while the 3-charge economy is still enforced for ordinary undos. Codex's proposed 'consume then restore on respawn' is strictly more fragile. Claude's panel correctly classified this as guarded.

findings.title: Rewind animates the moving platform over 0.2s instead of snapping, causing physics desync
findings.note: Overstated. platformPhase is restored to target.platformPhase on the same call (line 978), and updatePlaying re-writes movingPlatform.position.y = base + sin(phase*2)*amp EVERY frame — so the platform's Y is governed by the phase, not the .move action, and is correct from the next frame. The .move's X target equals movingPlatformBaseX (oscillation is Y-only), so its X write is a no-op. Net: the .move action is redundant/dead, not a desync source. No collision artifact. (Cleanup-worthy: drop the .move and snap, but no fairness/crash impact.)

findings.title: finalPlatformSurface recovered via children.first as? SKShapeNode (silent break risk)
findings.note: Currently correct: createPlatform adds exactly one child (the surface shape) before returning, so children.first IS the surface at capture time. It's a latent fragility — if createPlatform ever gains another first child the glitch telegraph + trap reset silently no-op (finalPlatformSurface is optional-chained everywhere, so it fails quiet, not crash). Best fix: have createPlatform return the surface explicitly. Polish, not a live bug.

findings.title: positionHistory grows unbounded on high-refresh devices
findings.note: recordPosition trims every frame by time (cutoff = gameTime - historyDuration 3.0s). At 120fps that caps the buffer at ~360 entries; it cannot grow unbounded. deepseek's own text concedes the time-trim 'prevents the worst case.' The proposed 300-entry cap is redundant. Non-issue.

findings.title: Instruction panel's three atmospheric lines never tell the player how to undo (first-encounter fairness)
findings.codeRef: Level16_ShakeUndo.swift:655-680 (atmospheric clue), :780-818 (JIT SHAKE TO UNDO prompt), :1158-1160 (hintText)
findings.note: Intentional discovery design, and well-escalated. The panel deliberately withholds the verb ('REGRET HAS A GRIP. USE IT.'); the verb is delivered by the 1.25s post-collapse JIT 'SHAKE TO UNDO' prompt (VoiceOver-announced) exactly when the player is stranded, plus the death-gated hintText() that names shaking. codex/gemini/kimi all judged this fair. Not a real fairness gap.

findings.title: isWideCanvas gate is 700 but the adjacent comment cites 760pt
findings.note: Real comment drift only. The 700 gate is correct (sits above the 430pt widest iPhone, below 768pt iPad portrait). The stale '760-pt width' sentence in the doc comment is the only defect. Cosmetic.

findings.title: tierCount double-clamped (16 in ipadFillTierCount, then 13 at call site)
findings.note: Cosmetic readability nit. The effective cap is 13 (the call-site min wins); the inner default 16 is dead. On real iPads the natural count is ~14 so the 13 clamp can shave one tier, leaving the ~60pt dead band noted in the iPad-void finding. Harmless but confusing — collapse to a single 13 cap.

findings.title: undoLabel.fontName bypasses VisualConstants.Fonts constant
findings.note: Minor token-consistency nit; the whole file uses hardcoded Menlo/Menlo-Bold for hand-built HUD labels (title uses VisualConstants.Fonts.display, the rest are literal). Cosmetic only.

findings.title: Worst-case zig-zag rung pairing (two 80pt rungs) is exactly 130pt edge-to-edge — zero margin
findings.note: Opposite-side rungs are 2*colOffset=210pt center-to-center. The narrowest climb width is 80, so an 80/80 pairing yields 210-40-40=130pt edge-to-edge = maxJumpableGap exactly (safe cap, NOT over absoluteMaxGap 145). All other width pairs are tighter. No fairness violation, but the 80-width entry leaves no timing margin on iPad; nudge climbWidths min to 84 to restore headroom.

findings.title: deltaTime/gameTime resume-spike could wipe the rewind buffer and waste a charge
findings.note: Investigated and DISMISSED. The base update() clamps dt to 1/30s before calling updatePlaying, so gameTime/platformPhase can never jump on background-resume; the time-windowed trim in recordPosition therefore never wipes the buffer from a spike. No edge bug. Recorded so the panel sees it was checked.

findings.title: didEnd schedules setGrounded(false) on leaving ANY ground even if still touching another platform
findings.codeRef: Level16_ShakeUndo.swift:1092-1103 (didEnd), :1077-1089 (didBegin sets grounded true on any ground contact)
findings.note: Theoretical: didEnd fires a delayed setGrounded(false) whenever Bit leaves a ground body, without ref-counting simultaneous ground contacts. On this level platforms are discrete (confined column + spaced phone course) so Bit never straddles two at once, and any re-contact re-sets grounded via didBegin. No observed effect here; flagged as a latent pattern, cosmetic for L16.

topActions: Refactor createPlatform to return/store the surface SKShapeNode explicitly so finalPlatformSurface no longer depends on children.first (removes the silent-break risk on the glitch telegraph + trap reset).
Drop the redundant movingPlatform.run(.move...) in performUndo and just snap position.y from the restored phase (updatePlaying already governs Y) — removes the competing-action smell gemini flagged.
Raise the minimum climbWidths entry from 80 to ~84 so the worst-case iPad zig-zag pairing has margin under the 130pt gap cap instead of sitting exactly on it.
Collapse the double tier clamp to a single cap and fix the stale comments (isWideCanvas 700-not-760; tierCount 13-not-16) so the iPad route reaches the ceiling with no confusing dead values.
contested: iPad dead-sky void (claude: real concern; current confined-column build largely fixes it — residual is ~60pt, cosmetic — claude reviewed a stale screenshot)
repairTrap not consuming a charge (codex: rough edge to fix; claude + the code's own softlock-guard comment: intentional and required for completability — code is the tiebreaker, codex wrong)
Moving-platform 0.2s move (gemini: severe desync; code shows updatePlaying governs Y from the next frame and the .move is effectively a no-op — gemini overstated, polish at most)

verdictLine: Shippable polish-tier level: clever death-plane Airplane Mode mechanic, completability and jump-reach legal on both phone and iPad paths; the only real residuals are an iPad first-paint void and a rare turbulence-flicker-vs-OFF fairness edge.

findings.title: iPad first paint reads as a broken/empty void in the OFF state
findings.note: With mode OFF only the 4 solids render (spawn x110/t0 bottom-left, REST x705/t4 far across, breath x1290, finale x1870) while all 12 flyers sit at groundY-220 in the death plane. On the wide iPad frame the middle band is empty and the lone REST outline floats orphaned, so the level looks incomplete until the player toggles. Claude's dashed ghost-silhouette of each flyer at its ON target Y is the strongest surviving fix and makes the puzzle intent legible on load. Real and worth doing; cosmetic-leaning polish, not a blocker.

findings.title: Turbulence wobble can flicker isGrounded false and bypass the death-plane OFF protection
findings.codeRef: updatePlaying() L685-698 turbulence; flyingPlatformSupportingBit() L584 guard bit.isGrounded; handleGameInput() L653
findings.note: Turbulence sets platform.position directly (+-1.5 X, +-2 Y) on the static body Bit stands on. didEnd schedules setGrounded(false) after 0.05s, so a wobble that briefly breaks contact flips isGrounded false. flyingPlatformSupportingBit() returns nil when isGrounded is false, so if a real system OFF (NWPathMonitor connectivity restored -> isEnabled:false) lands on that same frame, the protection at L612/L653 is bypassed and the platform under a stationary Bit drops into the hazard -> unfair death. Low probability (requires a wobble-induced ungrounding coincident with a network flip) but a genuine fairness hole. Deepseek's fix is correct and clean: make turbulence purely cosmetic by wobbling a child node and leaving the static physics body position fixed, so grounded never flickers. The panel raised the symptom but did not connect it to the OFF-protection bypass.

findings.title: Airplane Mode ON does not call notePlayerProgress(), so the stall-hint timer keeps counting after the core idea is solved
findings.codeRef: updateAirplaneState() L601-642 (no notePlayerProgress); base no-progress fallback BaseLevelScene L708-718
findings.note: Deaths call notePlayerStruggle() but the first ON toggle never resets the progress timer. A player who toggles ON, then carefully studies the now-revealed climb for ~22s, can still trip the base-class no-progress fallback and get the 'Turn ON Airplane Mode' hint spelled out after they already did it. Harmless but slightly dumb. One-line fix: call notePlayerProgress() the first time enabled becomes true in updateAirplaneState().

findings.title: iPad upper finale jumps are legal but near the ceiling (rise ~78 + gap ~65) and turbulence X-wobble eats margin
findings.codeRef: buildComposedIPadLevel() flyingSpec L311-324; verticalTier clamp BaseLevelScene L104
findings.note: Verified math on an iPad Air canvas: max forward rise 78.3 (<=85) and max edge gap 65 (<=130) across the whole route; verticalTier clamps rise to <=85 so smaller iPads only get easier. Legal. But the finale cluster pairs a near-max single-tier rise with a ~115pt center step while turbulence adds +-1.5pt X to the landing target, so those jumps feel unforgiving on a real device. Tightening the finale flyer X spacing by ~10-15pt or widening the t8-t11 flyers a touch would restore margin. Polish; not required to ship.

findings.title: platformDelayOffsets only covers 3 of the 12 iPad flyers, so the staggered cascade is lost on iPad
findings.note: Real observation but already handled intentionally: L24-28 and L618 explicitly guard the index and fall back to delay 0 for flyers past the table, documented as 'animation timing only, no gameplay effect.' Flyers 4-11 rise simultaneously instead of cascading. Purely a visual flourish; zero gameplay impact, comment proves it intentional. Extending the array to 12 entries is a trivial nicety, not a fix. Classifying as false-positive-as-a-defect (cosmetic at most).

findings.title: setupBackground() hardcodes cloud span 1945 instead of sharing composedCourseWidth
findings.codeRef: setupBackground() L87; composedCourseWidth set in buildComposedIPadLevel() L342
findings.note: The two values DO match (composedCourseWidth = 1885 + 60 = 1945; the comment at L87 even cross-references it). More importantly the reviewers' suggested fix is infeasible: configureScene() calls setupBackground() (L70) BEFORE buildLevel() (L72), so composedCourseWidth is still 0 when clouds are laid out. Sharing it would require reordering or a forward-declared constant. This is a maintainability nit (two literals that must stay in sync), not a bug. Cosmetic.

findings.title: OFF-debounce 'swallows deliberate OFF toggles' and feels unresponsive
findings.codeRef: handleGameInput() L653; GameRootView.swift L262; NetworkManager.swift L60-65
findings.note: There is no in-app OFF affordance. The accessibility button (GameRootView L262) and DebugInputPanel both post only isEnabled:true; the sole source of isEnabled:false is NWPathMonitor (connectivity restored). So the debounce only ever swallows a SYSTEM-generated OFF, which is exactly the death-trap it must guard against. The player never sends a 'deliberate OFF' through the game, so nothing feels unresponsive. Current code is the tiebreaker: kimi is wrong here.

findings.title: Keeping a platform aloft while mode is OFF creates an inconsistent visual/system state
findings.note: Intentional and documented: skipping the descent of the one platform Bit stands on prevents animating him into the hazard. It self-corrects on the next OFF once Bit steps off. The momentary one-platform-aloft state is strictly safer than the alternative (instant death) and is the explicitly designed anti-trap. Not a defect.

findings.title: First-encounter players may try jumping or hunt for an in-world button instead of toggling a system feature
findings.codeRef: showInstructionPanel() L519-540; createAirplaneIndicator() L454-460; accessibility button GameRootView L261; hintText() L739-741
findings.note: Mitigated by design: the top-right plane indicator labeled OFF plus the airplane glyph on each flyer give an explicit experiment target, the in-app accessibility airplane button exists, and escalation (notePlayerStruggle on death + the 22s base no-progress fallback) spells out 'tap the plane icon or swipe Control Center down.' The poetic t=0 trio deliberately de-spoils the trick (a prior consensus goal). This is the intended earned-reveal arc, not a fairness gap. Cosmetic.

findings.title: Hint escalation slams from poetry straight to a full Control Center tutorial with no intermediate nudge
findings.codeRef: notePlayerStruggle() BaseLevelScene L744-750; no-progress fallback L702-718; hintText() L739
findings.note: Partly mitigated by the base class: the time-based no-progress fallback (~22s, or ~18s timer) fires the same hint for a stuck-but-not-dying player, and the death path requires struggleCount>=2 AND 8s before showing it. So there IS pacing, just only one hint string. A genuinely intermediate nudge would be nice-to-have but the existing dual-trigger escalation is acceptable; the 'no override to pace help' claim is inaccurate given notePlayerStruggle and the fallback both exist. Cosmetic.

findings.title: deltaTime resume-spike and camera/IUO crash hazards on the camera-anchored HUD path
findings.codeRef: BaseLevelScene update() clampedDt L698; performConfigurationIfReady() L243-261; setupLevelTitle/createAirplaneIndicator/showInstructionPanel camera adds
findings.note: Checked for the runtime edges in scope and found them already safe: deltaTime is clamped to 1/30 in the base update before turbulenceTime accumulates, so a background-resume spike cannot launch platforms; gameCamera is guaranteed non-nil before configureScene() runs (set in performConfigurationIfReady/didMove behind the gameCamera==nil guard), so the camera-anchored addChild calls for the title/indicator/panel cannot trap; grounded uses a boolean (not a counter) so there is no contact-counter underflow. Reporting as a clean no-finding for the hunt categories.

topActions: Make turbulence purely cosmetic: wobble a child node inside each flying platform and leave the static physics body position fixed, so isGrounded never flickers and the death-plane OFF protection can never be bypassed mid-wobble (fairness).

Add a dashed ghost-silhouette of each flying platform at its ON/flying target Y while mode is OFF (critical on iPad) so the first paint reads as an intentional 'these will rise' reveal instead of a broken/empty void (polish).
Call notePlayerProgress() the first time Airplane Mode turns ON in updateAirplaneState() so the stall-hint timer resets once the core idea is solved (polish, one line).
Tighten the iPad finale flyer X spacing (t8-t11) by ~10-15pt or widen those flyers slightly to restore landing margin against the near-max rise+gap plus turbulence X-wobble (polish).
contested: kimi 'debounce swallows deliberate OFF toggles / unresponsive' — overruled: no in-app OFF affordance exists (only system NWPathMonitor sends OFF), so the debounce is purely protective. codex/gemini/claude treatment of the protection as intentional is correct.

codex/kimi 'share composedCourseWidth with the cloud span' — overruled on feasibility: setupBackground() runs before buildLevel(), so composedCourseWidth is 0 at cloud-layout time; the hardcoded 1945 already equals the computed extent. Cosmetic, not a bug.

kimi 'platformDelayOffsets covers only 3 of 12 flyers kills the cascade' — downgraded to cosmetic/false-positive: code comments (L24-28, L618) prove the index guard is intentional and animation-only with no gameplay effect.

kimi/deepseek 'escalation slams from poetry to tutorial with no pacing' — partially overruled: base-class no-progress fallback (~22s) and the struggle>=2 & 8s death gate both pace the single hint string; the claim of 'no override to pace help' is inaccurate.

verdictLine: Solid ship-quality level. The headline round-1 concern (broken iPad diagonal-staircase + camera-follow VOID + gameCamera-ordering UI bugs) is fully resolved in the current build — Level18 dropped installCameraFollow entirely and rebuilt the iPad path as a confined center-anchored zig-zag column with scene-fixed HUD. Worst-case iPad gap is 125pt edge-to-edge (within the 130 budget), so DeepSeek's "unreachable gap" finding is a false-positive (it conflated 210pt center-to-center with edge-to-edge). Only one mild real finding survives: the combined near-max diagonal jump (125pt gap + up to 85pt rise at once) is borderline on iPad, plus a cosmetic trajectory-line polish item.

findings.title: iPad diagonal-staircase climb + X-only camera-follow strands spawn/instruction and floats finale hazards (VOID)
findings.note: Current build deleted installCameraFollow on this path entirely (see the configureScene/build comments) and replaced the bottom-left->top-right staircase with a CONFINED vertical zig-zag column centered on size.width/2. Title, instruction panel, freeze overlay and timer are all attached to `self` at scene-fixed coordinates now, not to a scrolling camera. The whole floor->ceiling climb + every hazard fits one resting frame. The VOID root cause is gone.

findings.title: iPad zig-zag horizontal gaps exceed the 130pt edge-to-edge limit, making every other jump unreachable
findings.note: DeepSeek read 210pt center-to-center (2*colOffset=210) as an edge-to-edge gap and also cited a phantom '81/169pt' from the old build. Computed across tierCount 5..10 the worst real edge-to-edge gap is exactly 125pt (c2c 210 minus the two narrow 80-90pt rung half-widths), inside maxJumpableGap=130. The teach->first-rung and last-rung->finale steps are only 105pt c2c (edge gaps -10/+5, i.e. overlapping/trivial). Gemini, Kimi and Codex were right; the current code is the tiebreaker.

findings.title: setupLevelTitle/createPeekOverlay/showInstructionPanel touch gameCamera before installCameraFollow creates it, so HUD attaches to world space and scrolls off
findings.note: Two reasons this is wrong against current code: (1) Level18 no longer parents any HUD to gameCamera — title/overlay/panel all addChild to `self` at fixed scene coords. (2) Even if they did, BaseLevelScene creates gameCamera in didMove/performConfigurationIfReady BEFORE configureScene runs, with an explicit crash-fix comment. The cited line numbers (112/444/503) are from the old build.

findings.title: First encounter is unfair: player must discover swipe-up mid-air with no safe practice space and the panel never names the gesture
findings.note: Partly mitigated to the point of non-issue. Bit spawns standing on a 150pt-wide teach platform (beat 0) with only ONE slow, telegraphed spike (speed 1.0, hover 95) above it — the player can stand still indefinitely and observe; there IS safe practice space. The atmospheric panel intentionally does NOT name the gesture (correct de-spoil per the panel comment + Claude/Codex/Gemini agreement), and the explicit gesture reveal is delivered by the timed hint contract: standing idle triggers BaseLevelScene's 22s wall-clock no-progress fallback -> showDifficultyHintIfNeeded -> hintText() ('Swipe up slightly... just enough to peek the App Switcher'). That timed reveal IS the escalation. Severity-wise a mild discoverability tax remains but it is by-design and netted.

findings.title: hintText() only repeats the swipe-up instruction; no escalating struggle prompts via notePlayerStruggle to diagnose what the player is stuck on
findings.note: handleDeath() does call notePlayerStruggle() (line 782), and enterPeekMode() calls notePlayerProgress() (line 604) — the exact hint contract Codex confirmed. The 'single hintText' is intentional: this level's discovery hurdle is one gesture, so one escalation reveal is the correct design, not a bug.

findings.title: removeTrajectoryLines may leave orphaned nodes in the NEXT scene if a transition happens while lines are active
findings.note: SpriteKit scenes do not share a node tree; transitionToNextLevel() does GameState.load() which constructs a brand-new scene. Trajectory lines are children of the OLD scene, which is torn down — they cannot 'persist into the new scene.' Worst case is a one-frame visual nicety on the dying scene. Not a leak across scenes.

findings.title: physicsWorld.speed left at 0 / deltaTime spike on resume after backgrounding the peek
findings.note: Hunted explicitly. (1) Resume spike: BaseLevelScene.update clamps dt to 1/30s (clampedDt) and passes it to updatePlaying, which is what the peek countdown subtracts — no time-jump on app resume. (2) Stale speed=0: if the player reaches the exit mid-peek, the scene is replaced by a fresh one with its own physicsWorld, so speed=0 never carries over. exitPeekMode restores speed=1 on the normal path. No softlock.

findings.title: isWideCanvas (height>1000 && width>700) falls back to phone-ish layout on iPad landscape / split-view canvases
findings.note: Real but low-impact and consistent with every other level's L3-template gate (the game ships portrait-locked per the shared pattern). In iPad landscape or a narrow split-view the phone course centers via courseX with the 390pt design width and gameplayLift, which is a degraded-but-completable framing, not a crash. Worth a future stable min-dimension audit; not a ship blocker.

findings.title: showTrajectoryLines rebuilds dashed lines with a per-frame while-loop of many tiny segments (perf / frame drops on rapid peeks)
findings.note: Real but negligible: at most 3 (phone) or ~7-9 (iPad) hazards, each line ~120pt / (6+4)=~12 dash segments, rebuilt only on peek ENTER (not every frame — showTrajectoryLines is called once in enterPeekMode, then physics is frozen). Total is a few dozen path segments per peek. Gemini's CGPath(dashingWithPhase:lengths:) swap is a clean cleanup but not a measurable hit at this scale.

findings.title: showTrajectoryLines hardcodes a 120pt centered dashed line regardless of each spike's actual sweep range
findings.note: True: lineLength is a fixed 120pt rather than the spike's real range (40-60pt). The line slightly over-represents the danger sweep, which is conservative/forgiving for the player (it implies MORE coverage than the spike has), so it never causes an unfair death. Cosmetic accuracy nit only.

findings.title: startDesyncedOscillation nearly always picks goingRight:true because phase<1 is true for all phase except 1.0
findings.note: Misreads the design. `goingRight: phase < 1` only sets the FIRST leg's direction; the spike then self-reschedules with !goingRight each leg (line 439-440), so it oscillates fully both ways regardless. The initial position is also offset by phase*range so the start point is randomized across the sweep. The bias on leg 1 only affects which direction the very first short leg travels — immaterial because the spawn x is already randomized and full back-and-forth follows. No gameplay effect.

findings.title: Combined near-max diagonal jump on iPad (125pt edge gap + up to 85pt rise in one leap) is borderline-reachable
findings.note: The budget treats gap (<=130 edge) and rise (<=85 top-to-top) as separable, but each iPad climb rung jump must clear BOTH simultaneously: a 210pt c2c step (125pt edge gap) WITH a full one-tier rise. With apex ~91pt, a jump that spends ~85pt on rise has little arc left to carry 125pt horizontally — this is the hardest combined case in the level. It is mitigated when tierCount is high (per-tier rise drops below 85, e.g. ~80 on a 13' iPad), and the teach/finale/rest steps are trivial, so most pad pairs are fine; but on the minimum tierCount=5 device the mid rungs sit near the simultaneous gap+rise ceiling. Recommend nudging colOffset down to ~95 (edge gap ~105) OR widening the climb rungs to 95pt to buy timing margin on the diagonal. Borderline, iPad-only, not a hard violation.

topActions: iPad only: ease the combined diagonal jump on the zig-zag climb — drop ipadColOffset from 105 to ~95 (edge gap ~105pt) or widen climb rungs from 80/90 to ~95pt — so a full one-tier rise + horizontal carry isn't simultaneously near the budget ceiling on minimum-tier (e.g. 11') iPads.

Optional polish: replace the per-segment while-loop dashed trajectory with a single CGPath(dashingWithPhase:lengths:) and base lineLength on each spike's actual range instead of a hardcoded 120pt (Gemini + DeepSeek cleanups, cosmetic).
Optional future: add a stable minimum-dimension / landscape audit so an iPad in landscape or split-view doesn't silently fall back to the centered 390pt phone strip (Codex).
contested: iPad zig-zag gap: DeepSeek (exceeds 130pt, unreachable) vs Gemini/Kimi/Codex (safe). Current code is the tiebreaker — worst edge-to-edge gap is 125pt across all tier counts, so Gemini/Kimi/Codex are right and DeepSeek is a false-positive (conflated 210pt center-to-center with edge-to-edge).

First-encounter fairness: DeepSeek/DeepSeek/Kimi (unfair, no practice space, gesture never named) vs Claude/Codex (fair de-spoil with timed reveal). Tiebreaker: the teach platform is a 150pt safe stand with one slow telegraphed spike, and the 22s no-progress fallback surfaces the explicit swipe-up hintText — Claude/Codex are right; the de-spoil is intentional, not a fairness bug.

iPad VOID + camera/gameCamera-ordering: Claude/DeepSeek flagged it as broken; the 'Review-driven fixes' commit already removed installCameraFollow and made the HUD scene-fixed, so both are now already-fixed, not real-unfixed.

verdictLine: Solid and completable — the clever two-door / three-scan biometric trap is well-guarded, the decline-count software fallback prevents any hard biometric softlock, and jump math is inside budget on both phone and iPad. Surviving issues are polish-tier: the multi-scan structure is under-taught and the auth-confusion (non-death) path gets no escalating hint.

findings.title: isUnlocked stays false until step 3 while door2 blocker clears at step 2 — claimed unlock 'mismatch'
findings.note: Intentional, not a bug. The level is a THREE-scan design on TWO doors: door1 tapped once (step1), door2 tapped twice (step2 opens door2's blocker, step3 sets isUnlocked + 4th-wall). Removing the blocker at step2 lets the player WALK to the exit body, where handleExit's `guard isUnlocked` fires showExitNudge('ONE MORE SCAN' -> 'TAP THE NEXT GATE'), deliberately sending them back for the final scan. deepseek's proposed 'fix' (set isUnlocked=true in case2) would DELETE the third scan and the finale 4th-wall beat. The nudge points at door2 which is open but still needs one more tap — that is the designed third scan, not a dead pointer. handleExit is reachable and the nudge is throttled (isShowingExitNudge). Working as authored.

findings.title: Deaths do not call notePlayerStruggle(); no struggle-driven hint, only the generic wall-clock net
findings.note: Reviewed against an older build. CURRENT handleDeath() DOES call notePlayerStruggle() (line 950, with the 'Mirrors the sibling device scenes' comment). The 'Review-driven fixes' commit added it. All four reviewers were right about the OLD code and are now stale on this point. (A real residual gap survives separately — see the auth-confusion hint finding below — but the flat root-cause claim 'no notePlayerStruggle' is fixed.)

findings.title: No escalating hint for the non-death auth-confusion path (multi-scan / 'TAP THE NEXT GATE' ambiguity)
findings.codeRef: Level19_FaceID.swift:950 (struggle only on death), :799 & :977 ('TAP THE NEXT GATE'), :988-990 (hintText)
findings.note: notePlayerStruggle() now fires only on falling into the death zone. But the genuine stuck-state on THIS level is auth confusion, not falling: a player who reaches door2, taps it once (step2), then doesn't realize the SAME door must be tapped AGAIN for scan 3 never dies, so struggleCount never increments and the only escalation is the generic ~22s wall-clock net surfacing hintText() 'Authenticate identity' — which is vaguer than the t=0 panel and doesn't name the repeated/second gate. The Face-ID DECLINE path is well covered (declinesBeforeFallback=2 -> surfaceAuthFallback). The simple-confusion path is not. Lowest-cost fix: count a struggle (or upgrade the on-screen status) when handleExit's nudge fires repeatedly, and make hintText name the multi-scan ('the gate needs another scan').

findings.title: iPad composed path leaves a mid-screen void; gameplay crammed low, camera Y stays centered so the climb isn't visible
findings.codeRef: Level19_FaceID.swift:260-382 (buildComposedIPadLevel), BaseLevelScene.swift:136-140 (installCameraFollow keeps camera Y centered)
findings.note: The screenshot the round-1 panel saw was the OLD low-band iPad layout. CURRENT buildComposedIPadLevel authors absolute tiers from playableGroundY (bottomSafeY+90) up to playableCeilingY (topSafeY-150) via fillTierCount, which by construction spans the full usable band — the documented fix for exactly the 'low strip, top half empty' bug. Camera Y staying centered is the SHARED, intentional iPad convention across all sibling levels (vertical fill is handled by the tier band, not the camera; see the installCameraFollow doc-comment), not a Level-19 regression. The horizontal scroll for the wide course is correctly installed (line 625-627). Marking false-positive against current code; if any residual dead-sky remains it would be a shared-helper concern, not specific to this scene.

findings.title: Dead faceFrameWidth assignments (lines 207, 308) — createVaultDoor hardcodes width 50
findings.note: Confirmed real but inert. faceFrameWidth is declared, defaulted to 50, and reassigned to 50 in both build paths, yet createVaultDoor's inner faceFrame hardcodes `width: 50` at line 477 and never reads the property. Pure dead state — no behavioral effect, no visual difference (all values are 50). Delete the property and the two assignments, or wire 477 to use it. Cosmetic cleanup only.

findings.title: resolveFallbackName() fails to trim possessive when device name uses a smart quote
findings.note: Wrong file. resolveFallbackName()/UIDevice.current.name belong to Level23_DeviceName (the device-name mechanic), not Level19_FaceID. There is no name-resolution or possessive-trimming logic anywhere in FaceIDScene. gemini's finding does not apply to this level at all.

findings.title: faceIDDeclineCount increments on accidental cancel too; fallback surfaces after 2 cancels even if the player wanted Face ID
findings.note: This is the intended anti-softlock behavior, not a bug. surfaceAuthFallback only ADDS the on-screen ID button and repoints the status label; it never removes the real Face-ID gate. A player who actually wanted Face ID can still tap the door and get the real system prompt — the fallback is strictly additive. Surfacing it after 2 declines is the deliberate release-build guard against the 'IMPOSTER DETECTED forever' loop documented at lines 99-110. Strengthening it (e.g. pulsing earlier) is the polish suggestion below, but the current behavior is correct and safe.

findings.title: Make the on-screen fallback ID button pulse after the FIRST declined scan instead of waiting for two
findings.note: Legitimate accessibility polish. Players who need assistive access wait through two full 'IMPOSTER DETECTED' alarm cycles (~4s each) before the fallback appears. A gentle pulse/affordance after the first decline (or showing the button immediately but muted) would help assistive users discover it faster without weakening the gate. Optional; the level is already softlock-safe at 2 declines plus the wall-clock net.

findings.title: Second sequential gate (and the exit-behind-blocker trap) is undiscoverable until after step 1
findings.codeRef: Level19_FaceID.swift:593-605 (t=0 panel mentions only one vault), :799 ('TAP THE NEXT GATE' first appears post step-1)
findings.note: Mild legibility issue. The t=0 instruction panel ('WALK TO THE VAULT' / 'TAP IT TO SCAN YOUR FACE') intentionally teaches only the verb without spoiling the twist (a deliberate, good de-spoil choice). But the existence of a SECOND gate, and that the exit is trapped behind it, is only revealed after door1 opens. This is by design as a surprise beat; the polish ask is a small on-screen marker/arrow toward door2 once door1 opens so the two-gate structure reads before the player walks into the trapped-exit nudge. Overlaps with the auth-confusion hint finding (the strongest single fix addresses both).

findings.title: Redundant Face-ID re-prompt when proximity sensor is covered during an active system prompt (real device)
findings.note: On a real biometrics device, if the player covers the proximity sensor while the LAContext Face-ID prompt is already on screen, proximityFlipped(isCovered:true) takes the else branch and calls triggerFaceIDPrompt() again, stacking a redundant requestAuthentication. No correctness bug — the scanStep<3 guard in triggerFaceIDPrompt and advanceScanStep-only-on-success prevent any double-advance or over-unlock, and LAContext coalesces — but it can momentarily re-animate 'SCANNING...' and is slightly wasteful. Could guard with an 'authInFlight' flag. Cosmetic.

findings.title: exit-trap reachability math holds on phone and iPad
findings.note: Verified, no defect — recording as confirmation. Phone: door2 blocker spans logical x[355,415], exit body x[385,425]; Bit stopped at blocker left edge (355) reaches only ~355, 30pt short of the exit's left edge (385) while closed. iPad: 55pt blocker-center-to-exit-center offset (wider than phone's 20pt) plus a 120pt-tall blocker (top ~115pt above the finale platform, clearing Bit's ~91 apex). Both gates are genuinely un-jumpable and the second scan is mandatory. Completability verified: the software-fallback button posts faceIDResult(true) -> advanceScanStep, door-agnostic, so all three scans (and thus the exit) are reachable without any real biometric hardware.

topActions: Add an escalating hint for the non-death auth-confusion path: count a struggle (or upgrade the status label / 4th-wall nudge) when handleExit's 'ONE MORE SCAN' nudge fires repeatedly, and make hintText() name the multi-scan requirement instead of the vague 'Authenticate identity'. This is the single highest-impact fix and also makes the second-gate structure legible.
Add a small on-screen marker/arrow toward door2 the moment door1 opens, so the two-gate / trapped-exit structure reads before the player walks into the refusal nudge.
Pulse (or show muted) the on-screen fallback ID button after the FIRST declined Face-ID scan rather than waiting for two declines, so assistive-access players discover it ~4s sooner.
Delete the dead faceFrameWidth property + its two =50 reassignments (or wire createVaultDoor line 477 to read it) — cosmetic cleanup, no behavior change.
contested: isUnlocked step-2-vs-step-3 'mismatch' (deepseek): current-code tiebreaker says FALSE-POSITIVE — it is the intentional 3-scan/2-door design; deepseek's proposed fix would delete the final scan and the finale beat. deepseek was wrong.

'Deaths don't call notePlayerStruggle' (claude/codex/kimi/deepseek): all four were right on the OLD build, now ALREADY-FIXED at line 950. They were correct historically; current code is the tiebreaker and shows it resolved. A narrower residual (no hint on the non-death auth-confusion path) does survive.

iPad mid-screen void (claude/codex): current-code tiebreaker says FALSE-POSITIVE — the composed path now spans the full playableGround..playableCeiling tier band, and centered camera-Y is the shared intentional iPad convention. Panel saw the pre-fix screenshot.
resolveFallbackName smart-quote bug (gemini): FALSE-POSITIVE for Level 19 — that method lives in Level23_DeviceName, not this scene. gemini reviewed the wrong mechanic.

verdictLine: Solid, completable, App-Store-safe finale; the panel's death-driven-hint worry is already neutralized by the base-class idle fallback, and the only real survivors are two cosmetic iPad-rhythm bugs (skipped peak beat, removeAllActions clobbering the cleared animation) plus an 11-inch landscape mis-route.

findings.title: Escalating hint is death-driven only; a player who stops at the wall never dies and never earns hintText()
findings.note: The base update() has a WALL-CLOCK no-progress fallback: if !hintShown and Date()-lastProgressAt >= noProgressFallbackDelay (~22s), showDifficultyHintIfNeeded() fires hintText() regardless of any death. lastProgressAt is stamped at play start (line 490) and Level20 never calls notePlayerProgress() before the wall, so a stuck-at-the-wall, never-dying player DOES get the 'push Bit INTO the blocks' hint after ~22s. This is the idle-nudge escalation the round-1 reviews predated. Concern is grounded but resolved.

findings.title: First-encounter unfairness: 'IT WANTS YOU TO TURN BACK' reads as 'avoid the wall', players will backtrack instead of pushing in
findings.note: Overstated against current code. The purge auto-fires on PROXIMITY > 0.6, which is distanceToCorruption < 80pt from wall center (not contact) — comments compute reachable proximity 0.77/0.786. So a player merely WALKING UP NEAR the wall triggers the purge automatically; they need not deduce 'push into it.' Plus the physics-contact trigger in didBegin and the ~22s idle hintText backstop. The psychological read is real but the mechanic self-resolves on approach, so it is not a fairness blocker. A pulsing 'push' arrow (kimi) is a nice-to-have, not required.

findings.title: peakIndex = min(7, top) is skipped by the loop 'while idx < top' when top == 7, so the isolated PEAK beat (120-gap) never spawns
findings.note: Correct catch: when top==7 the loop runs idx=5,6 and stops before idx=7, so the peakIndex=7 stone is never created — the tier-7 spot is instead filled by the wide run-up (apW at tier(top)). Climb stays CONTINUOUS and fully jumpable (one tier per stone, then the +110 void to the run-up), so it is NOT a completability/jump-reach break — only a lost rhythm beat on canvases where top lands at exactly 7. Cosmetic. Fix: clamp peakIndex = min(7, top-1) or special-case the peak so it always falls inside the loop range.

findings.title: hintLabel.run(clearedSequence) immediately followed by hintLabel.removeAllActions() cancels the 'CORRUPTION CLEARED' animation
findings.note: Real. The just-queued fadeOut/recolor/fadeIn/scale sequence is wiped by removeAllActions() on the next line (intent was to stop the repeatForever pulse from line 639). Result: the hint label snaps to green '✓ CORRUPTION CLEARED' with no animation. Not a softlock (label still updates), but the celebratory beat is dead. Fix: move removeAllActions() BEFORE the new run(), or drop it and let the new non-repeating sequence override the pulse.

findings.title: isWideCanvas (height>1000 && width>700) mis-routes iPad Pro 11-inch landscape (1194x834) into the phone path
findings.note: Accurate: an 11-inch iPad in landscape is 834 tall, under the 1000 threshold, so it gets buildPhoneLevel() (the simple centered strip) instead of the composed climb. The phone path is fully COMPLETABLE on that canvas (it just letterboxes the strip), so no crash/softlock — only a less-impressive layout on one orientation of one device class. The comment frames the threshold as deliberately phone-safe, but it does also catch landscape 11-inch iPads. Polish: gate on a UIUserInterfaceIdiom == .pad check or a lower height threshold if the composed climb is wanted in landscape.

findings.title: showFakeCrashScreen camera-cleanup fails silently if gameCamera not initialized, leaving black overlay permanently on screen
findings.note: Wrong. The overlay is parented to the camera ONLY inside 'if isWideCanvas, let cam = gameCamera' (line 796), and on iPad installCameraFollow set gameCamera in setupBit before any contact. Cleanup enumerates BOTH the scene AND gameCamera (lines 869-872), so whichever parent holds the overlay, it is removed. If gameCamera were nil the overlay would have been added to the scene, which the scene-enumerate catches. No permanent-overlay path exists.

findings.title: Post-purge exit gap is exactly 130pt with zero margin for physics/contact variance
findings.note: 130 is the SAFE budget cap (maxJumpableGap), not the hard cap (145), so this is at-budget by design with 15pt of headroom before the documented hard limit — Bit clears it. Intentional, not a violation. Tightening to ~115-120 would add felt margin (worth doing as polish) but it is not a completability risk.

findings.title: Design drift: guide says app deletion/reinstall but code performs an in-app simulated purge (keychain only used for prior-clear shortcut)
findings.note: Accurate but a docs/copy concern, not a code defect — the App-Store-safe simulated purge is the correct call (claude agrees the conversion was wise). Update the level guide / in-world copy to 'system purge simulation' so the device-feature mapping matches. No gameplay impact.

findings.title: hasShownFakeReview latches true on first purge; if the player misses the crash screen it never replays
findings.note: DeepSeek itself flags this as intentional. The guard is the re-entrancy protection that prevents stacked crash overlays from repeated physics contacts before isCleared flips ~6s later. Once triggered the purge runs to completion and clears the gate, so there is nothing to 'miss' that would block progress. Correct as-is.

findings.title: Re-entrancy / idempotency of the purge trigger across the dual physics+proximity path
findings.note: Verified safe: beginSimulatedPurge() guards on '!isCleared && !hasShownFakeReview' and sets hasShownFakeReview first, so the physics-contact and proximity-0.6 paths cannot both run the sequence. claude listed this as a STRENGTH; confirming it holds in current code.

topActions: Fix the removeAllActions() ordering in executeCorruptionClear() (move it before hintLabel.run, or delete it) so the 'CORRUPTION CLEARED' animation actually plays — the only survivor with visible player-facing impact.

Fix the skipped PEAK beat: clamp peakIndex = min(7, top-1) (or special-case so the peak always lands inside the 'while idx < top' loop) so the isolated 120-gap landmark spawns on top==7 canvases; pure rhythm polish, climb is already completable.
Tighten the isWideCanvas gate (idiom == .pad, or a lower height threshold) so an 11-inch iPad Pro in landscape gets the composed climb instead of the phone strip.
contested: Death-driven hint (claude/codex/gemini said unfixed): current code's base-class ~22s wall-clock fallback fires hintText() for a stuck, never-dying player — the reviewers predated the idle-nudge commit; status already-fixed.

First-encounter unfairness (gemini/deepseek/kimi said real): the proximity>0.6 auto-trigger fires within 80pt of the wall center on mere approach, so the player need not deduce 'push in' — overstated; status false-positive/polish.
Camera-cleanup leaves a permanent black overlay (deepseek): cleanup enumerates both scene AND camera, so the overlay is always removed — deepseek wrong; status false-positive.
130pt post-purge gap 'zero margin' (claude/codex/kimi): 130 is the SAFE cap (hard cap 145), so it is at-budget by design, not a violation — claude/codex/kimi overstated severity.

verdictLine: ship — completable on every device (no softlock); 1 new fragile-clamp/squeeze fairness-polish issue on iPhone bay3, ~4 false-positives, identity-naming cosmetic, spoiler is by-design

findings.title: iPhone bay3 reachability proof is numerically wrong; Bit squeezes against pillar2 but still contacts door3 exit
findings.note: The clamp comment claims maxX = courseX(430)-11 = 379, using HALF the real body width. PlayerController uses halfWidth = character.size.width/2 = 22 (body is 44 wide) and boundaryPadding 20, so real maxX on a 390pt iPhone is 368, not 379 (375->353, 393->371). At that clamp Bit's 22pt physics body [357,379] straddles solid pillar2 [351,360] AND overlaps the 30pt-wide door3 exit body [358.7,388.7] from 358.7..379 — so door3 (the rightmost real door, reachable via hash%3) STILL completes on every iPhone width, but only because the exit body is wide; Bit visibly jams against pillar2 at the clamp. Kimi's 'physics squeeze' was directionally right (though wrong that it's a softlock or that bays are < the 44pt body — the body is 22pt). Recommendation: fix the comment's math and either nudge worldWidth ~+12 or shift the rightmost slot/pillar left a few pt so Bit seats cleanly in bay3 instead of relying on exit-body overlap while pinned to a pillar.

findings.title: Doppelganger can solve the puzzle / opens a door on a timer
findings.note: The doppelganger is a plain SKNode following a CGPath with NO physicsBody (grep confirms only platforms/pillars/exit/death have bodies). It cannot trigger PhysicsCategory.exit; rejection/spotlight labels are cosmetic SKLabelNodes on a timer. The armed exit is gated solely by Bit's physics contact, independent of the doppelganger. No bug; the 'race is decorative' point is a design opinion, not a defect.

findings.title: resolveFallbackName returns a non-uppercased raw string, inconsistent with normalizedName
findings.note: scheduleNameFallback feeds resolveFallbackName() INTO updatePlayerName(), and updatePlayerName runs playerName = normalizedName(name), which uppercases. So the raw return at line 194 is normalized/uppercased before display. Case is consistent. Also door matching is by hash-derived realIndex, not string compare, so even a mismatched display case could not break completion.

findings.title: resolvedFallbackRealX/DecoyX set in buildPhoneLevel but never updated after assignDoorIdentities
findings.note: These constants are ONLY the .first(where:) fallbacks in realExitPosition/doppelgangerTargetPosition for the case exitDoors is empty. assignDoorIdentities always marks exactly one isRealExit=true door, and the doppelganger race starts 3s AFTER name resolution, so the real/decoy door NODES always exist by then and the fallback X is never used. Dead-but-defensive; no wrong-position bug.

findings.title: No escalating hint chain; stuck players stall (hintText returns same line forever)
findings.note: BaseLevelScene has BOTH a struggle-count path (fires showDifficultyHintIfNeeded at 8s after the 2nd death) and a wall-clock no-progress safety net (~22s since last progress). handleDeath() calls notePlayerStruggle() and hintText() returns a non-nil mechanic hint, so a stuck player gets escalating help. The 'stall forever' claim does not hold against current base class.

findings.title: Final door bays are narrower than Bit's body, causing a physics squeeze that can trap the player
findings.note: Bit's COLLISION body is 22pt wide (size 44 * 0.5), not 44pt. iPhone center bay2 = 27.2pt, iPad center bay = 40pt — both clear the 22pt body. No trap/softlock. The genuine narrow-clearance pain is the bay3/pillar2 clamp interaction captured in the NEW finding above; the generic 'bays < 44pt body' framing is wrong.

findings.title: Instruction + narrator spoil the device-name solution before the player discovers it
findings.note: 'FIND THE DOOR WITH YOUR NAME' + 'THE DOOR KNOWS TOO' is an intentional 4th-wall teach for a one-shot device-feature beat; the doppelganger twist is NOT spoiled. This is a design-taste opinion (4 reviewers raised it as preference), not a correctness/fairness bug. Optionally soften by fading door labels in after the name resolves (DeepSeek's suggestion), but nothing is broken.

findings.title: Identity fracture: filename Level23, header 'Level 23', but levelID index 21 and title 'LEVEL 21'
findings.note: File is Level23_DeviceName.swift and the doc header says 'Level 23', but levelID = LevelID(world:.world3,index:21) and the on-screen title is 'LEVEL 21', and inline comments mix both numbers. No runtime impact (the level plays as 21), but it makes bug-tracing error-prone. Recommendation: reconcile the filename/header/comments to the canonical index 21.

findings.title: normalizedName mishandles trailing-apostrophe / smart-quote possessives ("James' iPhone", curly quotes)
findings.note: Only the ASCII "'s " pattern is stripped. "James' iPhone" or a smart-quote ’s yields a label like "JAMES' IPHONE" instead of "JAMES". Purely a display-label aesthetic on the matched door; the level is still completable (the displayed name is matched to its own armed door, not parsed). Low priority; a small regex covering ’/‘ and trailing s' would clean it up.

findings.title: 5s name fallback can fire before the player registers the device-name was the point
findings.note: On simulator / no Local-Network-permission, the .deviceNameRead event may not arrive and the 5s fallback resolves to 'PLAYER' (or a parsed owner), so the 'the OS knows your real name' moment can quietly degrade. It's a deliberate anti-softlock net (correctly guarded by nameReceived) and the level stays completable, so this is polish only. Codex's suggestion (gate the fallback behind an explicit 'name unavailable' beat) would preserve the device-feature moment; optional.

topActions: Fix the iPhone bay3 clamp: correct the wrong half-width math in setupBit (line 908-916 uses 11, real is 22 -> maxX is 368 not 379) and nudge worldWidth ~+12 or shift the rightmost slot/pillar a few pt left so Bit seats cleanly in bay3 instead of completing only via exit-body overlap while jammed on pillar2
Reconcile the Level 21 vs 23 identity (filename/header/inline comments) to the canonical levelID index 21 to stop mis-tracing

verdictLine: ship — 1 real fairness fix worth doing (FLY gate vs transient bridge state, missed by all 5), 2 minor real polish items, 5 false-positives (incl. a wrong-file keypad "crash"), 0 crashes/softlocks

findings.title: FLY gate keys off transient bridgeExtended, which auto-retracts after 5s — a legitimately-crossed player gets the wrong 'SPEAK BRIDGE AND OPEN FIRST' rejection
findings.codeRef: activateFly() line 803; retract at extendBridge() lines 753-758
findings.note: guard bridgeExtended && doorOpened uses the LIVE bridge flag, which flips back to false 5s after BRIDGE is spoken (auto-retract). A deliberate player who says BRIDGE, walks the ~227pt span, then reaches the FLY point past the cleared door more than 5s later finds bridgeExtended==false, so FLY is rejected with a misleading 'SPEAK BRIDGE AND OPEN FIRST' even though they already crossed. Recoverable (re-say BRIDGE from the far side to re-set the flag, or the base no-progress fallback shows hintText within ~22s), so fairness not softlock — but it is a real first-encounter trap none of the 5 reviewers caught precisely. Fix: gate FLY on a LATCHED 'bridge has been extended at least once' flag (parallel to flyUsed), not the transient bridgeExtended.

findings.title: Bridge 5s auto-retract has no visual warning and can strand a hesitating player mid-chasm
findings.note: Real: the 5s retract fires on an invisible clock; a first-timer who walks slowly after BRIDGE falls to the death zone with no telegraph. Not a softlock — respawn works and the struggle/no-progress hint escalates — so polish. Recommend fading the bridge alpha as it nears retraction or a brief 'BRIDGE HOLDING' cue. Note this compounds the new FLY-gate finding above; both are downstream of the same transient-retract design.

findings.title: iPhone locked door keeps 90pt height (top ~+105) vs iPad 107pt — ~1pt over-clearance allows a frame-perfect skip on iPhone only
findings.codeRef: buildPhoneLevel() line 233 (door at groundY+60, default height 90); iPad line 386 uses 107
findings.note: Real and asymmetric. Door center groundY+60, height 90 -> top groundY+105. Bit body bottom at apex ~groundY+106 (apex ~91 above the platform-top stance at groundY+15), i.e. ~1pt above the door — a max-height frame-perfect jump can clear OPEN's door on iPhone. The iPad path deliberately raised the blocker to 107pt for exactly this margin (per the line 386 comment), so the iPhone path was left with the unsafe margin. Low player impact (requires near-perfect input and only skips one of three gates) but it is the gating mechanic. Fix: pass height:107 on the iPhone door too.

findings.title: Keypad node-name split-by-underscore will crash if the random code contains '_'
findings.note: False-positive / wrong file. There is no keypad, no handleKeyTap, and no name-splitting anywhere in this scene (grep confirms zero matches for handleKeyTap/split/userData). Kimi and Gemini reviewed a different level's keypad logic and mis-filed it under VoiceCommand. Ignore.

findings.title: hintText() dumps the full command order immediately with no escalation; struggle only fed on death
findings.codeRef: hintText() line 1123; BaseLevelScene notePlayerStruggle() lines 744-750, update() lines 703-718
findings.note: False-positive against current base infra. hintText() is just the string source — it is NOT shown immediately. It surfaces only via showDifficultyHintIfNeeded(), gated on struggleCount>=2 AND >=8s playtime, OR the no-progress timer (noProgressHintDelay), OR a ~22s wall-clock fallback. So the order is revealed only after the player demonstrably struggles, exactly the escalation the reviewers asked for. deltaTime is also clamped (line 698) so the resume-spike class is handled.

findings.title: JUMP alias for FLY can be triggered accidentally while experimenting aloud
findings.codeRef: handleGameInput case FLY/JUMP lines 1020-1021; gate in activateFly() line 803
findings.note: Effectively guarded. Saying JUMP/FLY at spawn (before BRIDGE+OPEN) hits the activateFly() gate and only shows 'SPEAK BRIDGE AND OPEN FIRST' — no impulse, no mechanic fired, no skip. The alias cannot short-circuit the puzzle. At most a cosmetic stray hint; not worth changing.

findings.title: doorBlocker! force-unwrapped in createLockedDoor
findings.note: False-positive. doorBlocker is assigned a fresh SKNode on line 509 immediately above and given a physics body on 510-512; the force-unwrap on 513 can never be nil in this synchronous path. No crash risk.

findings.title: Instruction-panel fade relies on brittle //permissionContinueButton recursive poll that never self-terminates if the base renames the node
findings.note: Low-stakes and self-documented. The poll only defers a COSMETIC 5s fade of the instruction panel. If the base ever renamed permissionContinueButton, permissionOverlayPresent would return false and the fade would simply run on its normal timer — the panel never gets stuck on screen and nothing gameplay-affecting breaks. A delegate/notification would be cleaner but this is not a bug.

findings.title: Fallback BRIDGE/OPEN/FLY buttons pinned at hardcoded y=50, ignoring bottomSafeY
findings.note: Minor real cosmetic. Buttons are placed at screen y=50 regardless of the home-indicator inset; on devices with a tall bottom safe area they sit a touch low but the 30pt-tall buttons remain on-screen and tappable. Only appears on the no-mic fallback path. Recommend anchoring to bottomSafeY+something; low priority.

findings.title: No-mic fallback buttons reveal all command words and flatten the puzzle
findings.note: False-positive against current code. The reveal is gated on micPathFailing (VoiceCommandManager.micDenied OR no command recognized yet by the 6s tick). A player on a working mic who has landed even one command (hasSpokenFirst true) never sees the buttons — the in-scene reveal is explicitly suppressed (see the COSMETIC FIX comment lines 869-883). So the words are only exposed when speaking is impossible, which is correct accessibility, not a leak.

findings.title: iPad tier step ~83pt sits only ~2pt under the 85pt rise cap
findings.codeRef: buildComposedIPadLevel() tierCount=14 line 321; verticalTier clamp BaseLevelScene line 104
findings.note: False-positive. ~83pt is within the SAFE budget (maxJumpableRise 85, absoluteMaxRise 91) by design, and verticalTier() additionally clamps step to maxJumpableRise so it can never exceed 85 even if the band math drifted. The margin is intentional, not marginal-unsafe. Every consecutive iPad platform changes tier index by at most 1, so each rise is one safe step.

findings.title: bridgeExtended/doorOpened stay true after retract/open, could confuse a player who re-triggers
findings.codeRef: extendBridge() line 732 guard, openDoor() line 778 guard
findings.note: doorOpened latching true is correct (the door does not re-lock). The deepseek note conflates this with confusion but identifies no actual defect; the real issue in this area is the OPPOSITE — bridgeExtended does NOT latch and flips back to false on retract, which is the genuine FLY-gate finding logged separately above. As stated, this is a non-issue.

topActions: Latch FLY's prerequisite on a 'bridge has been extended at least once' flag instead of the transient bridgeExtended, so a player who crossed before the 5s retract isn't wrongly told to 'SPEAK BRIDGE AND OPEN FIRST' (activateFly line 803).
Raise the iPhone locked-door height from the 90pt default to 107pt to match the iPad margin and close the ~1pt frame-perfect skip-over of the OPEN gate (buildPhoneLevel line 233).

Telegraph the 5s bridge retract (fade the bridge as it nears retraction or a brief 'BRIDGE HOLDING' cue) so a hesitating player understands the mid-chasm fall instead of dying to an invisible clock (extendBridge lines 753-758).

verdictLine: Solid, completability-safe level — the "drain to win" inversion is airtight (hidden exit inert until <60%, can't be cheesed); the only real survivors are first-encounter discoverability of the DRAIN button and death-gated hint escalation, both fairness-not-crash.

findings.title: Hint escalation is death-gated only — a stuck-but-not-dying player never gets nudged toward DRAIN
findings.note: notePlayerStruggle() is reached ONLY through handleDeath(). The level's whole challenge is non-lethal confusion: the player bounces off the fake EXIT? door and wanders, never dying, so struggleCount never increments and the targeted hint ('Lower your battery %') never fires. notePlayerStruggle also requires struggleCount>=2 AND >=8s play, so even death-driven escalation is slow. Fix: add a time/idle/fake-exit-touch trigger that calls notePlayerStruggle() independent of death (e.g. after the player touches fake_exit_trigger twice, or after ~10s with battery still >=60%). This is the single highest-impact survivor.

findings.title: First encounter never signals that DRAIN POWER is the answer
findings.note: The t=0 panel ('SOME THINGS ONLY APPEAR WHEN YOU'RE RUNNING LOW / MORE POWER ISN'T ALWAYS BETTER') correctly telegraphs the inversion without spoiling, and the DRAIN POWER button is always on-screen so the level IS completable (this refutes deepseek's 'uncompletable on simulator' claim). But nothing links the inversion to the button or to draining as an action — the player can read 'running low' as something to avoid. Lower-cost than building new art: fold this into the escalation fix above (the hint text already says 'lower your battery %'); making that hint reach a non-dying player largely closes this gap too.

findings.title: DeepSeek: level uncompletable on simulator if player never taps DRAIN
findings.note: The DRAIN POWER button is created unconditionally in configureScene() and is always visible/tappable (touchesBegan routes to simulateBatteryDrain). Simulator posts a constant 75% (BatteryLevelManager defaults -1 -> 75), but the button is the explicit, always-available completion path. It is a discoverability problem (covered above), not uncompletable. No auto-drain is needed for completability.

findings.title: resizeStone width mismatch when a vanished iPad stone reappears (rebuilt at hardcoded 45x18)
findings.note: iPad stones authored wider than 45 (e.g. 50% plateau w=160) keep the wide visual surface but, on reappearance, updateBatteryState rebuilds the physics body at the default 45x18 — narrower than the drawn lip, so a player standing near the visual edge falls through. UNREACHABLE on the simulated path (simulateBatteryDrain is monotonic-decreasing and once simulatedBattery!=nil real events are ignored at line 743, so stones never reappear). Only reachable on a real device that recharges past a threshold mid-level. The code comment at 369-373 acknowledges it and calls it cosmetic; it is slightly more than cosmetic (collision lip) but narrow. Fix: persist each stone's authored width and rebuild from it.

findings.title: iPad 40% stone and 50% plateau overlap horizontally by ~42.5pt
findings.note: Confirmed: 40% surface spans [537.5,612.5], 50% spans [570,730] -> 42.5pt X overlap. BUT they sit on different tiers (tier 2 vs tier 4), so they are vertically separated — no physics intersection, no reach problem, just visual crowding on the scrolled iPad course. Claude was right it exists but overstated it as a layout concern; it is cosmetic. Optional: nudge the 40% stone left or shrink it.

findings.title: dimOverlay brightness snaps instantly on each battery step (no color animation)
findings.note: updateBatteryVisuals assigns overlay.fillColor immediately rather than via an SKAction.colorize/fade, so each 10% DRAIN tap is a hard brightness jump. Purely cosmetic polish; the snap arguably reinforces the 'power dropped' beat. Optional .colorize(with:..., duration:0.2).

findings.title: Codex: permanent drain button reduces a real battery mechanic to an in-game knob
findings.note: This is intended design, not a defect: the drain button is the simulator/no-real-drain fallback (the same pattern other device levels use) and, on a real device, handleGameInput still honors live battery until the player opts into simulation. Not a bug to fix.

findings.title: Kimi/Codex: isWideCanvas gate also fires on iPad landscape (portrait-authored course)
findings.note: The gate requires height > 1000. iPad landscape heights are ~820-1024 depending on model and are generally < the portrait-only 1000 threshold, so landscape falls to buildPhoneLevel() (the lifted/centered phone band), NOT the composed climb. The composed portrait course is therefore deliberately prevented from rendering in landscape. Intentional and documented in the comment; not a broken-layout bug.

findings.title: simulatedBattery! force-unwrap (line 726)
findings.note: Guarded: line 725 assigns simulatedBattery = max(0, ...) (always non-nil) immediately before the force-unwrap on 726, so it cannot trap. Harmless.

findings.title: Delayed setGrounded(false) on any ground-contact-end uses a boolean, not a contact counter
findings.note: didEnd schedules setGrounded(false) after 0.05s on ANY ground contact ending. Where stones/platforms are adjacent (e.g. the iPhone 10-stone band, edge-gaps as low as 5-7.5pt on iPad), leaving one platform while still standing on the next can schedule a false setGrounded(false). Mitigated by (a) the 0.05s delay, (b) PlayerController coyote time (PlayerController.swift:74-112), and (c) the next platform's didBegin re-asserting grounded — and this is the shared idiom used by sibling levels, so it is not a new regression here. Flagged for completeness; impact is at most a rare 1-frame jump-eligibility blip, not a softlock. No change recommended unless the shared pattern is reworked globally.

topActions: Make struggle/hint escalation non-death-gated: trigger notePlayerStruggle() on a timer/idle threshold or after the player touches the fake exit ~twice, so a stuck-but-not-dying player is nudged toward DRAIN POWER (closes both the escalation and discoverability gaps).

Persist each iPad stone's authored width and rebuild its physics body from that value in updateBatteryState() instead of the hardcoded 45x18, to remove the visual-vs-collision lip when a wide stone reappears after recharge.

Optional polish: nudge/shrink the iPad 40% stone to remove the 42.5pt visual overlap with the 50% plateau, and animate the dimOverlay color change (SKAction.colorize) so DRAIN taps fade rather than snap.
contested: DeepSeek's 'uncompletable on simulator' — REJECTED: the always-present DRAIN POWER button is the intended completion path; it's a discoverability issue, not uncompletable.

Codex/Kimi 'isWideCanvas fires on iPad landscape' — REJECTED: the height>1000 gate excludes landscape, so landscape gets the phone layout by design; current code is the tiebreaker, the comment confirms intent.
Codex 'drain button reduces the real mechanic to a knob' — REJECTED as a defect: it's the documented fallback and live battery is still honored until the player opts into simulation.
Kimi 'simulatedBattery! force-unwrap' — REJECTED: provably guarded by the non-nil assignment on the preceding line.

verdictLine: ship — 0 real crash/softlock, 1 minor polish survivor (pre-arm purge nudge), the rest already-fixed or false-positives (iPad void fixed, struggle-hint wired, cache-delete is intentional teardown)

findings.title: iPad renders as a diagonal void; terminal/junk/exit off-screen
findings.note: Claude's round-1 review was against the OLD build. Current code removed installCameraFollow (setupBit lines 858-864 confirm no follow), camera rests at scene center (BaseLevelScene:279), and buildComposedIPadLevel authors a confined zig-zag column at climbCenter=size.width/2-78 ±48. Verified on-screen: junkX=w/2+130, exitX=w/2+185, terminal ledge near ceiling, spawn near bottom — all fit one 744..1024-wide portrait frame. The void is gone. No action.

findings.title: Deaths don't call notePlayerStruggle(), so stuck players get no faster help
findings.note: handleDeath() now calls notePlayerStruggle() (line 1021); base impl (BaseLevelScene:744-750) escalates to the level hint after >=2 struggles and >=8s. Codex/gemini reviewed the older build. Already wired.

findings.title: Junk blocks keep physics during 0.8s scatter and can collide with the player mid-air
findings.note: The scattering junkBlocks are decorative SKShapeNodes with NO physicsBody (createJunkMass never assigns one to them). All collision comes from the single invisible junkBlocker, whose category is zeroed at line 873 BEFORE the scatter, then clearGroundedIfStandingOn drops the player off it. Animating the visual blocks cannot collide. kimi was wrong — current code already handles solidity correctly.

findings.title: removeCacheFile() in willMove deletes the cache prematurely / breaks Settings solve path
findings.note: Intentional and guarded. deactivate() (runs on app backgrounding) explicitly does NOT delete the file (commented at SM:59-64) so the Settings/free-storage solve works while backgrounded; removeCacheFile() runs ONLY on true scene teardown (willMove), when the player has left the level. Re-entering re-activates and recreates the file. No premature deletion in the real solve flow.

findings.title: armPromptLabel = panel.children.first as? SKLabelNode is always nil (dead field)
findings.note: Correct: panel's first child is the bg SKShapeNode (line 648), so the cast yields nil and the armPromptLabel?.removeFromParent() guard at 622 is dead. Harmless because showArmFeedback runs once (armTerminal is guarded by !terminalArmed). Just delete the unused field/line; no player impact.

findings.title: Stale comment at lines 514-516 references camera-follow that was removed
