findings.note: Correct: the GUARD comment says the iPad wall is 'camera-followed' and at 'an absolute X far past size.width', but camera-follow was removed and the wall now sits at w/2+130 (on-screen). The code (the !isWideCanvas guard at 517) is still correct — only the rationale text is stale. Doc-only cleanup.

findings.title: t=0 text spoils the objective by naming 'PURGE TERMINAL'
findings.note: Naming the terminal is the intended discoverability cue, not a spoiler: the intro names WHERE to go ('JUMP TO THE PURGE TERMINAL FIRST') but withholds the actual device trick (free storage / CAN'T DO THIS?) until the terminal is armed (lines 613-618). That is the deliberate two-stage reveal. The other four reviewers correctly read this as a strength. Not a defect.

findings.title: First-encounter: tapping CAN'T DO THIS? before arming does nothing visible
findings.note: A pre-arm purge already gets feedback: warning haptic + black vignettePulse + flashTerminalHint() (terminal glow double-pulses) — it points the player back at the terminal. It's adequate but subtle; the panel never explains WHY nothing happened. Optional polish: a one-line 'ARM THE TERMINAL FIRST' toast on the rejected tap would close the loop. Low priority — intro panel + hintText already cover it.

findings.title: isWideCanvas 700pt threshold undocumented for in-between sizes
findings.note: min(width,height)>=700 is the project-wide tablet gate (matches BaseLevelScene:829 registerPlayer and the playableGroundY size.height>1000 split). It's a consistent convention, not an arbitrary local magic number. No behavioral risk.

findings.title: Add an 'ARMED' green indicator on the terminal
findings.note: Already present in spirit: on arming, the idle pulse is removed and the glow strokeColor cycles to VisualConstants.Colors.accent (lines 601-607) plus a select() haptic — a clear 'now armed' state change. A literal extra light is a nice-to-have, not a gap.

findings.title: screenSpaceCenter marquee placement with no camera follow
findings.note: Verified safe: camera is created at (w/2,h/2) and never moves (follow removed), so screenSpaceCenter resolves to true scene center on both devices. The JUNK PURGED marquee and camera-parented panels render at the visible center. No off-screen-juice regression.

topActions: Optional polish: when attemptPurge() is rejected for lack of arming (line 933-939), surface a brief 'ARM THE TERMINAL FIRST' toast so a player who taps CAN'T DO THIS? early understands why nothing dissolved — current haptic+vignette+glow-flash is good but silent on the reason.
Trivial cleanup: delete the dead armPromptLabel field/assignment (lines 622,673 — always nil) and fix the stale camera-follow comment at lines 513-516. Doc/lint only, zero player impact.

verdictLine: ship — geometry/completability solid on both devices; 1 real fairness edge (enemy snap-on-day), 2 polish (30s clock re-fire spam + guard-not-centered on phone), the rest false-positives/already-fixed

findings.title: Hint only fires on death, not for a living/hesitant player
findings.note: Round-1 was against an older build. Base update() now has BOTH a noProgressTimer (~18s) and a wall-clock fallback (~22s from last progress) that call showDifficultyHintIfNeeded() while alive, independent of notePlayerStruggle(). cycleTimeOverride() also calls notePlayerProgress() so the hint won't nag once the mechanic is engaged. Stalled-but-alive players DO get escalating help. No action.

findings.title: Override persists and blocks the real clock from taking effect / not reset across attempts
findings.note: By design. CYCLE TIME is the manual override that decouples the puzzle from the device clock so the level is ALWAYS solvable (that is the level's reason to exist). handleGameInput only auto-applies the clock when overrideMode==nil — intentional. 'Reset on exit' is moot: reaching the exit ends the level, and a replay re-instantiates the scene with overrideMode=nil/toggleIndex=0. Respawn (playBufferDeath) only repositions Bit in the same scene, so persisting the chosen mode through a respawn is the desired behavior, not a bug.

findings.title: Enemy teleports to patrol origin on switch back to DAY, can hit player unfairly
findings.note: Real but narrow. On a NIGHT/secret->DAY transition a sleeping spike is removeAction'd, hard-snapped to patrol.origin, and re-armed to .hazard in the same frame. Because the guard's origin sits on P2's landing (where the player stands after crossing in NIGHT), if the player cycles all the way back to DAY while standing there, the spike can materialize on top of Bit = an instant death with no visible approach. Low practical reach (requires the player to tap night->secret->day after already crossing, and they have no reason to). The snap is deliberate (avoids patrol center drift per the comment), so don't remove it — but ease/move the spike back over ~0.15s, or re-arm the hazard mask only after it has eased home, to remove the teleport-onto-player frame. Polish-to-fairness; lowest priority of the survivors.

findings.title: Guard spikes sit on the left shoulder of P2, not centered over the only landing — puzzle may read as a timing/position dodge
findings.codeRef: Level25_TimeOfDay.swift:359-363 (phone enemyData: guard center 250, range 35 -> sweep [215,285]) vs P2 span [220,310]
findings.note: Phone-only first-encounter clarity. The guard's awake DAY sweep covers [215,285] while P2's full top is [220,310], so the right ~25pt of P2 ([285,310]) is never swept — a first-timer can read 'just land far right of the spikes' and beat the forced jump without ever switching to NIGHT, weakening the required-mechanic read. The level is still completable and the intended solution still works; this is a teach-clarity gap, not a softlock. Fix: widen the guard (range ~45) or shift its center right so the awake DAY sweep makes EVERY viable P2 landing lethal, converting it from a dodgeable beat into an unambiguous must-sleep gate. The composed-iPad guard (range 70 over the full pad span) already does this correctly; only the phone path is loose. Highest player-impact survivor.

findings.title: showFourthWall()/narrator re-fires on every CYCLE TIME tap (spam)
findings.note: Partially real, broader than reviewers saw. GlitchedNarrator.present() replaces the existing line cleanly (Narrator.swift:174-175) so there is NO node leak/stacking — but the whisper line and its typewriter animation DO restart on every mode change. Worse than the button case: with no override, the device clock posts clockTimeUpdate every 30s (TimeOfDayManager:23) and handleGameInput calls applyTimeMode with NO hour-change guard, so the whole mode is re-applied AND showFourthWall re-fires every ~30 seconds (and the secret-hour .boss line re-fires every 30s at real 3:33 AM). Cosmetically intrusive, not a crash. Fix: guard handleGameInput on `hour != currentHour` before re-applying, and present the fourth-wall aside at most once per mode-enter (e.g. only when the resolved mode actually changed).

findings.title: Background clock icons hardcoded at x up to 560 scroll off-screen on iPad
findings.codeRef: Level25_TimeOfDay.swift:97-106 (setupBackground, scene-child icons at fixed x, alpha 0.1, z -10)
findings.note: True but cosmetic only. The five alpha-0.1, z=-10 decorative clock faces are scene children at fixed x=80..560; on the camera-scrolled iPad course they scroll out of view and don't tile the wider canvas. They are pure background texture, not gameplay, so no fairness/completability impact. Optional polish: parent them to the camera (like the HUD/ghost decorations already are) or tile across composedCourseExtent.

findings.title: Force-unwrap of overrideMode! at line 910
findings.note: Provably safe: overrideMode is assigned a non-optional modes[toggleIndex] on line 904 and force-unwrapped on line 910 with no intervening mutation. Cannot trap. Style nit only — could pass modes[toggleIndex] directly to applyTimeMode — but not a defect.

findings.title: Always-visible CYCLE TIME button makes the real-clock level trivially solvable / button easy to miss
findings.note: Intentional and necessary, not a flaw. Without an in-game override, the level would be unsolvable for any player whose real clock is in DAY unless they leave the app to change device time — a softlock the override exists to PREVENT. codex's 'make it a struggle-gated fallback' would re-introduce that exact softlock risk. deepseek's 'players may not notice it (bottom-right, small)' is mitigated: the instruction panel introduces the threat, and hintText() (wired to the escalating hint system) explicitly says 'Tap CYCLE TIME until it reads NIGHT.' Discoverability is acceptable; leave as-is.

findings.title: Phone P1->P2 forced gap is right at the 130pt budget with little margin
findings.codeRef: Level25_TimeOfDay.swift:174-182 (P1 span [10,90], P2 span [220,310] -> open gap 130)
findings.note: Within spec. 130pt edge-to-edge is exactly the documented safe-gap cap (maxJumpableGap=130), and the rise is ~12.5pt (vs 85 cap). 'Little margin' is acknowledged in the source comment but it is a deliberate single forced teach-jump, not a violation. Completable. No change.

findings.title: Contradictory instruction-panel width comments (300 vs 340)
findings.note: Stale comment only. The code uses width 340 (line 612); the earlier comment block at 599 still references a 300 value from a prior pass. Zero runtime impact — just reconcile the comment to avoid future-author confusion.

findings.title: isWideCanvas / threshold constants are arbitrary hardcoded magic numbers
findings.note: Documented, deliberate device-class gate matching the shared L3 template (the doc-comment at 52-56 explains the intent and why phones stay byte-identical). Consistent with verticalTier/playableGroundY which use the same size.height>1000 idiom. Maintainability nit, not a bug.

topActions: Phone guard clarity: widen the phone GUARD (enemy 0, line 359) range to ~45 or shift its center right so the awake DAY sweep covers ALL of P2's top [220,310] — turns the forced jump from a position-dodgeable beat into an unambiguous must-sleep-it gate (the iPad guard already does this).

Throttle the clock/narrator: add a `hour != currentHour` guard in handleGameInput (line 917-921) and present showFourthWall() only on an actual mode change, so the whisper/boss narrator stops re-firing (and re-running typewriter) every 30s from the device-clock tick.

Soften the DAY-transition spike snap (applyDayMode line 691-701): ease the spike back to patrol.origin over ~0.15s and re-arm its .hazard mask only after it arrives, removing the one-frame teleport-onto-player that can deal a no-warning death.

verdictLine: ship — completability/reach verified on both layouts; 1 real polish (iPad t=0 reads as broken void), 1 cosmetic (post-solve revert never rescrambles), the rest already-fixed or false-positives

findings.title: Stuck-but-alive player on rest pad never gets a hint (escalation only on death)
findings.note: The base update() has a wall-clock no-progress fallback: if !hintShown and Date().timeIntervalSince(lastProgressAt) >= noProgressFallbackDelay (~22s, longer with extendedHintTimers), it shows the hint EVEN with zero deaths and zero notePlayerStruggle() calls. lastProgressAt is stamped at play start (line 490) and on every notePlayerProgress(). So a player idling on the rest pad DOES get escalating help. Claude/kimi/deepseek were reviewing the old build; this is handled at the base layer. No level change needed.

findings.title: Revert-to-baseline after solving leaves signs permanently unscrambled (early-return makes revert branch unreachable)
findings.note: Kimi is technically right: the early-return `if language == baseline { return }` preempts the `!changedFromBaseline && isUnscrambled` revert branch, so rescrambleTextOnly()/rescrambleWorld() never run on a same-case revert and the signs stay readable. BUT consequence is purely cosmetic: the solve path (changedFromBaseline && !isUnscrambled, requires language != baseline) is NEVER blocked, puzzleLatched keeps the correct route solid, and the exit stays reachable. So no softlock/fairness impact — it just means the post-solve atmospheric re-scramble doesn't fire. Optional polish: drop the early-return and let puzzleLatched drive text-only rescramble.

findings.title: Locale identifier en-US vs en / raw == vs lowercased() inconsistency could misclassify a change
findings.note: currentLanguageCode returns Locale.current.language.languageCode?.identifier — the PRIMARY code only ("en","ja","de"), never a regional "en-US". Both baselineLanguage and the posted language come from this same property, so they are byte-identical case and form. The raw-== early return and the .lowercased() changed-from-baseline check can never disagree for real inputs. Codex's region/case concern does not manifest. (Normalizing is harmless defensive style, not a bug fix.)

findings.title: Nil-baseline risk: if currentLanguageCode is nil, changedFromBaseline falls back to 'en' and could auto-solve on launch for non-English devices
findings.note: currentLanguageCode is a non-optional String that already defaults to "en" internally, so baselineLanguage is never nil after configureScene. Even if it somehow were, the early-return at 670 ignores the baseline post, and the solve requires a language DIFFERENT from baseline. There is no launch-time auto-solve path.

findings.title: Cold-boot baseline drift: changing device language while app is killed re-baselines so switching back won't solve
findings.note: The kimi/gemini concern about persisting baseline to UserDefaults: the puzzle solves whenever the device language moves AWAY from whatever it was when THIS level instance loaded. A player who already changed language pre-launch simply changes it again (to anything different) to solve — there is no requirement to return to a specific prior language. The in-memory baseline is the correct semantics for a per-attempt puzzle; persisting it would actually break re-attempts. Not a defect.

findings.title: iPad t=0 reads as a broken empty void — hidden staircase is alpha=0/non-solid so the vertical center is dead and the exit floats isolated top-right
findings.note: On iPad the composed full-height climb's correct route is fully invisible until solved, so the launch frame shows spawn+wrong-cluster low, a tall blank band, and an isolated exit plateau at the ceiling — it reads as broken rather than 'a route waiting to be revealed.' The puzzle is still solvable and fair, but first-impression readability suffers specifically on the wide canvas. Fix: give hidden stairs a faint low-alpha ghost outline (keep categoryBitMask=.none so still non-solid) so the band reads as intentional. Phone layout is unaffected (overlapping narrow column hides the void).

findings.title: 137.5pt iPhone dead-end gap / 120pt iPad switchback span not marked off-limits — players waste lives testing the wrong route
findings.note: Verified geometry: iPhone last-wrong-pad -> exit plateau is 137.5pt (>130 safe gap) and the iPad wrong w3->wend rises 2-8 tiers (un-jumpable). These ARE the intended dual-route trap — a tantalizingly-close dead-end is the level's core puzzle, not a fairness bug. The 120pt iPad switchback span (verified max across tiers 8..14) is the CORRECT route and is within the 130 safe budget, so it is fully jumpable. Marking the wrong route 'off-limits' would defeat the lure. Hint escalation (death + 22s stall fallback) covers genuinely-stuck players.

findings.title: DEBUG test button toggles only ja/en — won't trigger a solve if baseline is neither
findings.note: The current toggle is baseline-aware: testLang = isUnscrambled ? baseline : (baseline == "ja" ? "en" : "ja"). For ANY non-ja baseline it posts "ja" (guaranteed != baseline) and for a ja baseline it posts "en" — so it always differs from baseline and always triggers a solve. Deepseek reviewed the older hardcoded version. Also DEBUG-only, never ships.

findings.title: Solving while standing on a wrong platform de-solidifies footing under Bit, dropping him to the death plane
findings.note: NEW edge: in unscrambleWorld() wrong platforms fade 0.3s then set categoryBitMask=.none. If the player changes language while standing ON a climbed wrong pad (e.g. iPhone w1 at g+80, or iPad w2/w3), there is no hidden pad directly beneath (hidden pads sit ABOVE), so Bit falls to the hazard plane. Mitigations that cap severity: (a) 0.3s fade gives a brief window, (b) handleDeath RESPAWNS at spawn — no softlock, no lost progress, and puzzleLatched keeps the route solved so the retry is trivial, (c) most players solve from spawn/rest, not mid-wrong-climb. Low-impact; optional fix is to delay wrong-platform de-solidify until Bit is not resting on it (groundNode check) — not worth blocking ship.

findings.title: deltaTime spike on resume causing physics jump
findings.note: Checked the runtime/state-edge class: the base update() clamps dt to 1/30s before physics/timers, so a backgrounding/resume spike cannot teleport Bit or fast-forward the hint timers. No level-local issue.

topActions: iPad polish: give the hidden staircase a faint low-alpha ghost outline (keep physics non-solid) so the t=0 wide-canvas frame reads as 'a route waiting to be revealed' instead of a broken empty void — the only survivor with real player-facing impact.

Optional cosmetic: drop the language==baseline early-return and let puzzleLatched drive rescrambleTextOnly() on revert, so the atmospheric re-scramble actually fires after solving (currently a no-op).

Optional fairness hardening: in unscrambleWorld(), gate each wrong-platform de-solidify on Bit not currently resting on it, so a solve performed while standing on the wrong cluster can't drop him (self-recovers via respawn today, so low priority).

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock, 1 real polish (rapid-toggle alpha overlap), 1 minor real polish (first-encounter cue), the rest false-positives or already-handled; iPad clarity is the only judgment call

findings.title: forceHardwareFallback(.voiceOver) never cleared → leaks into later levels
findings.note: DeepSeek's prescribed fix calls clearHardwareFallback — which does not exist (forcedFallbacks is insert-only). The 'leak' is inert: needsFallbackUI is gated by activeMechanics, which registerMechanics() RESETS per level, and only L27 ever registers .voiceOver. A stale .voiceOver entry in the global Set never surfaces in any other level. No action; if cleanup is desired, add a real removal API, but it changes nothing player-facing.

findings.title: First-encounter opacity: t=0 panel doesn't name the accessibility/VoiceOver fix; players may tap or hunt for a mic
findings.note: Unanimous, and partly true: the deliberately cryptic plaque ('named aloud') is intentional theming and the escalation IS wired — deathCount>=2 shows 'PHASE THE PATH IN FIRST', notePlayerStruggle()->hintText() explicitly names VoiceOver + the bottom-left button. So a stuck player is rescued within ~2 deaths. Residual gap is only the pre-first-death window. Low-cost win: have the death>=1 (not >=2) or a ~6s idle nudge point at the purple accessibility button by name. Polish, not a softlock.

findings.title: iPad climb is a ~250pt centered zig-zag column on a 1024+pt canvas → large blank L/R margins read as underfilled
findings.note: Claude alone, and it's a genuine visual-fill regression vs the design goal, NOT a fairness/completability bug — vertical fill is correct (tier0..tier13 spans floor→ceiling). The ±58 column was a deliberate fix for an EARLIER 'climb stacks off-screen right under dead sky' bug (see comment 337-345), so widening must preserve edge-to-edge gaps <=130. Recommend widening dx toward ±180-220 and verifying gaps, OR accept the ribbon. Cosmetic-leaning polish.

findings.title: Rapid VoiceOver on/off can leave real-stone surfaces mid-fade (alpha overlap between showRevealedState and dimRevealedState)
findings.codeRef: VoiceOverScene.swift:823-826 (showRevealedState removeAllActions), 861-866 (dimRevealedState sets alpha directly, no removeAllActions)
findings.note: Kimi is right that an overlap exists, but partly mitigated: showRevealedState() already calls removeAllActions() on surface+glyph before its fades, and dimRevealedState removes decoyPulse by key. The residual is dimRevealedState setting real-stone alpha=1.0 WITHOUT cancelling an in-flight fadeAlpha(to:1.0,0.25) from a just-fired showRevealedState — a mid-flight fade can briefly override it. Self-corrects in <0.25s; real stones stay solid/collidable throughout (physics unaffected). Cheap fix: removeAllActions() on real surfaces at the top of dimRevealedState. Cosmetic flicker only.

findings.title: systemReduceMotion is read but never used to suppress decoy pulse
findings.note: Already fixed/wired. showRevealedState() branches on systemReduceMotion: when true it sets static alphas (surface 0.35 / glyph 0.6) and SKIPS the repeatForever decoyPulse entirely; only the else-branch pulses. DeepSeek reviewed an older build. No action.

findings.title: setupBit() hardcodes verticalTier(0, of: 14) independently of tierCount
findings.note: Harmless: tier 0 == ground + 0*step regardless of count, so the duplicated literal 14 cannot desync the spawn from the build's floorY (both resolve to playableGroundY). It's a coupling smell worth a shared constant, but zero behavioral risk. Cosmetic at most.

findings.title: decoyPositions comment claims decoys are 'capped to keep it fair' but no cap exists
findings.note: True that the comment overstates, but it's a stale-comment nit with no gameplay effect: the helper returns exactly one decoy per interior gap (count-1), which IS bounded and fair for the 2/4/6 real-stone counts. Not worth a code change; trim the comment if touching the file.

findings.title: finale→exit gap mislabeled 76pt in comment when edge-to-edge is ~54pt
findings.codeRef: VoiceOverScene.swift:412 (exit dx +58 from S10 dx -58 → c2c 116, e2e 76)
findings.note: DeepSeek's own number is off: S10 is dx -58, exit dx +58 → center-to-center 116, minus two 40-wide half-stones (stone 40w, platform 84w → 20+42=62 half-sum) ... regardless both 76 and 54 are well under the 130 budget, so completability is unaffected either way. Comment-accuracy nit, cosmetic.

findings.title: hardcoded wide-canvas threshold min(size)>=700
findings.note: Standard project idiom; iPad path is correctly gated and the iPhone path is byte-identical below it. The shared verticalTier/playableGroundY helpers independently guard on size.height>1000, so the two thresholds agree for real devices. Not a bug.

findings.title: didEnd ground-contact delayed setGrounded(false) can un-ground Bit mid-stand during fast stone-to-stone hops
findings.note: NEW: didEnd schedules setGrounded(false) after 0.05s (coyote-time idiom). If Bit leaves stone A and lands on B within that window, A's delayed action still fires setGrounded(false) after B's didBegin set it true, briefly mis-flagging airborne. This is the standard shared pattern across levels (not L27-specific) and the 0.05s window plus immediate didBegin re-grounding make a missed jump unlikely with the generous ±58 gaps here. Low player impact; flag only because the prompt asks for animation-vs-physics desync class. Not worth a per-level fix.

topActions: Lower the explicit-cue threshold: surface the 'phase the path in via the bottom-left accessibility button' nudge on the FIRST death (or a ~6s idle), not death #2, to close the pre-first-death opacity window without spoiling the cryptic plaque.

(Optional, visual) Widen the iPad zig-zag column from dx ±58 toward ±180-220 and widen REST/BREATH platforms so the climb fills the 1024+pt canvas instead of a thin centered ribbon — re-verify every consecutive edge-to-edge gap stays <=130 at the larger offsets.
(Cheap robustness) Add removeAllActions() on real-stone surfaces at the top of dimRevealedState() to kill any in-flight reveal fade on rapid VoiceOver on/off toggles (cosmetic flicker only).

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock, 3 real polish (iPad goal not telegraphed, share-cancel has no on-screen feedback, single all-at-once hint); 4 false-positives (underscore collision, brute-force, force-unwrap crashes, struggle-coverage all disproven/already-fixed); 1 new cosmetic node-mismatch with no player impact

findings.title: iPad finale + locked door are off-screen at spawn; signature mechanic never telegraphed
findings.note: Correct: the iPad course is camera-scrolled (installCameraFollow, worldWidth = composedWorldWidth) and the SHARE/door finale sits at tier 11 far right, so spawn shows only an empty switchback staircase. No persistent 'TRANSMISSION AHEAD' arrow or camera-pinned door preview exists. Recommend a small camera-anchored '→ TRANSMISSION' marker so the climb's payoff is visible from spawn. Real but low-stakes (level is still completable and the climb itself reads).

findings.title: Share-sheet cancel leaves terminal with no explanation (no keypad, no status text)
findings.note: Grounded and NOT a softlock: on completed==false the SHARE button is never removed (only showKeyboard() removes it) so the player can immediately retry, and notePlayerStruggle() now fires to escalate the hint ladder — a later commit already added that struggle call. What's still missing is diegetic feedback. Recommend setting a terminal status like 'SHARE CANCELED — STILL ENCRYPTED' in the else-branch. Codex's exact suggestion stands; the other reviewers overstated it as a stranding bug, which the persistent button disproves.

findings.title: Underscore delimiter collision in keyBtn_<char>_<i> name parsing
findings.note: False-positive. The keypad symbols are drawn only from AirDropManager.alphabet = "ABCDEFGHJKLMNPQRSTUVWXYZ23456789" (AirDropManager.swift:59) which contains NO underscore, so a key char can never be '_' and parts[safe:1] always yields the intended glyph. Additionally a private Array[safe:] subscript (lines 983-988) prevents any out-of-range crash. The userData suggestion is a harmless refactor, not a fix.

findings.title: Force-unwraps will crash if setup order/parsing drifts (terminalScreen!, keyboardNode!, keyNode.name!, randomElement()!)
findings.note: Already-fixed in current source. createTerminalScreen/createInGameKeyboard build into local NON-optional refs (screen/keypad) and only publish to the optional property at the end (explicit comments say 'crash-proof'); keyName is via optional binding (let keyName = keyNode.name); alphabet.randomElement() uses '?? "X"' (line 120). DeepSeek reviewed the older build.

findings.title: Single hintText() dumps the full step-by-step solution instead of stepped nudges
findings.note: Partly real. Escalation IS gated (struggleCount>=2 && playtime>=8s in base notePlayerStruggle, plus a ~22s wall-clock fallback), so the full reveal is earned, not instant. But there is only ONE hint string and it spells out the entire loop including the keypad-decoy caveat. A two-stage ladder (first nudge: 'the terminal is the actionable object — try SHARE'; second: the current full text) would be fairer. Minor.

findings.title: Instruction panel never points at the actionable SHARE button (first-encounter fairness)
findings.note: Mild and partially mitigated: the SHARE button has a forever-breathe pulse to pull attention (suppressed under Reduce Motion, with a VoiceOver label), and the t=0 panel + escalating hintText cover the gap. Still, the panel text ('TAKE ME SOMEWHERE I CAN') is indirect for a first-timer. The same first-stage-nudge fix above resolves this. Low priority — not a completability risk.

findings.title: Salted keypad is brute-forceable
findings.note: False-positive. The answer is an ORDERED 6-symbol sequence over up to 12 keys (~12^6 ≈ 3M, repeats allowed and typeable since a key isn't consumed), and any wrong 6th tap fully clears enteredCode with a 0.6s reject beat. Mashing is infeasible; the design comment at 626-628 explicitly states the answer is hidden until shared so 'tapping every key is not a solution.' Intentional.

findings.title: notePlayerStruggle only fires on wrong-submit and death (insufficient hint coverage)
findings.note: Already-fixed. Current code also calls notePlayerStruggle() on a cancelled share (line 577), and the base update() has a wall-clock no-progress fallback (lines 703-718) that surfaces the hint even if struggle is never called. Coverage now spans wrong-submit, death, share-cancel, and pure idle.

findings.title: unlockDoor passes the wrong node to clearGroundedIfStandingOn (doorContainer vs doorBlocker)
findings.note: New, but no player impact. didBegin sets sharedGroundPlatform = groundNode(fromContact:) which returns the BODY-owning node — that's the child 'doorBlocker' (an SKNode added to 'door'), not 'doorContainer'. So clearGroundedIfStandingOn(doorContainer) can never match the guard sharedGroundPlatform === node and is a silent no-op. Harmless because the door is 120pt un-jumpable and is unlocked from the keypad while Bit stands on the platform, never on the door — Bit is never grounded on the door at unlock time. Cosmetic dead-code mismatch; pass doorBlocker (or door) if ever made standable.

findings.title: isWideCanvas hard-codes the size threshold instead of a base helper
findings.note: Cosmetic non-issue. The threshold intentionally matches the base helpers' own gate (gameplayVerticalLift/playableGroundY/verticalTier all gate on size.height>1000, BaseLevelScene.swift:38,73,101), so the layout branch and the geometry helpers agree by construction. The added size.width>designWidth guard is correct. No bug.

topActions: Add diegetic share-cancel feedback: in the completionWithItemsHandler else-branch set the terminal status to 'SHARE CANCELED — STILL ENCRYPTED' (Codex's suggestion) so a dismissed sheet reads as intentional, not broken.
iPad only: anchor a small camera-pinned '→ TRANSMISSION / DOOR AHEAD' marker so the scrolled finale + signature mechanic are telegraphed from the empty-staircase spawn.

Split the single hintText() into a two-stage ladder (first nudge points at the SHARE button / terminal-is-actionable; second is the existing full walkthrough) so the first-encounter fairness gap closes and the full answer stays earned.

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock, 1 real cosmetic-but-thematic fix (hesitation inflation), 1 polish (deltaTime camera), 1 minor polish (walk-back waypoint); 4 false-positives, the DeepSeek "stranded by cancel()" and "fakeExit off-screen" scares both disproved by current code.

findings.title: hesitationCount inflates during the reveal cutscene, corrupting the TRUST readout
findings.codeRef: Level29_TheLie.swift:813-824 (updatePlaying hesitation block); snapshot read at showPlayerAnalysis():694-735
findings.note: updatePlaying keeps running through the whole reveal sequence (~4.8s of forced stillness: 0.8+3.0 in triggerFakeExitReveal, +1.0 before showPlayerAnalysis). triggerFakeExitReveal calls playerController.cancel() so Bit is held still, and standingStillTime accrues deltaTime every frame, adding ~2 phantom hesitations BEFORE the analysis snapshot reads hesitationCount. notePlayerProgress() in revealTruth resets struggleCount/noProgressTimer but NOT hesitationCount or standingStillTime. Net effect: the 'we measured your doubt' gotcha shows a near-deterministic inflated number, undermining the level's payoff. No gameplay impact. Cheap fix: guard the hesitation block with `guard !hasReachedFakeExit else { standingStillTime = 0; lastPlayerX = bit.position.x; return }` (or only count while control is active and Bit grounded). Codex/Kimi both right.

findings.title: updateCamera lerp is frame-rate dependent (no deltaTime scaling)
findings.note: Fixed 0.1 smoothing per frame means the camera converges ~2x faster on 120Hz ProMotion than 60Hz — a feel inconsistency over the long right-then-left traversal, not a gameplay break (base update() already clamps dt to 1/30 so no resume spike). Gemini correct. Optional fix: frame-rate-normalize, e.g. factor = 1 - pow(0.9, deltaTime*60). Low priority.

findings.title: Long walk-back from fake exit to spawn has no mid-course confirming waypoint
findings.codeRef: Level29_TheLie.swift:633-672 (GO BACK arrow + chevron, camera-pinned); platforms only shaken not removed at 609-617
findings.note: After reveal the player walks ~1000pt (iPhone) / ~1570pt (iPad) left to the spawn door. The '<< GO BACK' label + '<<<' breadcrumb are pinned to the camera (always visible), and the original platforms remain as footing, so this is NOT a softlock or dead corridor — it is partially mitigated already. Residual polish: a single mid-course world-space '<<' marker (or a brief re-glow of the real door) would confirm the return trip is intended. Claude/Codex's core 'is this broken?' worry is softened by the camera-pinned arrow. Nice-to-have.

findings.title: playerController.cancel() supposedly never re-enables control and strands the player
findings.note: cancel() only resets touchMoveDirection/activeTouchCount/jumpBufferTimer/primaryTouchDidDrag — it is a one-shot input flush, NOT a latching disable flag. There is no isControlEnabled gate anywhere; update() keeps running every frame and the very next touchBegan re-arms movement/jump normally. The player is never stranded. DeepSeek wrong.

findings.title: Camera clamp leaves the fake exit off-screen on iPad so player walks toward something invisible
findings.note: Camera targetX clamps to levelWidth - size.width/2, which centers the viewport on x range [levelWidth-size.width .. levelWidth]. fakeExitX = levelWidth-50 sits 50pt inside the right edge — fully visible during the approach. The clamp prevents showing past the world edge, it does not hide the goal. DeepSeek (and Gemini's similar line) wrong.

findings.title: Lazy levelWidth/worldWidth won't update if canvas size changes after configureScene
findings.codeRef: Level29_TheLie.swift:50 (lazy levelWidth), :528 (worldWidth); BaseLevelScene.swift:243-261 (one-shot config), :441-447 (didChangeSize no rebuild); project.yml:43-44 (portrait-only)
findings.note: App is locked to UIInterfaceOrientationPortrait, and didChangeSize only calls performConfigurationIfReady() which is guarded by hasConfigured (runs once) — geometry is never rebuilt. The lazy levelWidth is correctly LOCKED to the absolute platform positions that were actually built; if size ever did change, keeping worldWidth matched to the built geometry is the safe behavior, not a bug. No clamp failure possible. DeepSeek wrong.

findings.title: iPhone jump reach is punishing — 50pt gap + 30pt rise consumes most of Bit's air, spike at x=600 taxes the same window
findings.note: Computed gaps/rises: max edge-to-edge gap 60pt, max top-to-top rise 30pt — vs the 130/85 budget, i.e. ~46% and ~35% of budget. These are comfortable jumps, not margin-eating. The x=600 spike oscillates y 210->250 above a single 530->660 crossing and is timed/avoidable (1.4s period) — a mild intentional 'rhythm tax' fitting a disguised-normal-platformer, not unfair. Kimi overstated.

findings.title: iPad vertical-tier climb collapses to a flat strip (verticalTier returns ground when size.height <= 1000)
findings.note: Hunted this as a NEW iPad-landscape regression: in landscape (height<1000) every tier would collapse to groundY, flattening the advertised diagonal climb (and even then the level stays completable — I computed all collapsed gaps <=100.6pt). BUT the app is portrait-locked (project.yml), so on every shipping iPad size.height is the portrait height (mini 1133, 10.2in 1080, 11in 1194, 12.9in 1366) — all >1000 — and the climb renders as intended. The >1000 guard IS the portrait-iPad gate. No real issue; documented so the panel knows it was checked.

findings.title: Reveal reads as an ambush, not a puzzle — first-timers have no visual reason to doubt the fake exit
findings.note: This is the level's entire thesis ('YOUR DOUBT WAS THE MECHANIC'). The deliberately-too-insistent subtitle 'NO GIMMICK. JUST WALK.' is the tell, and the earned hint 'Are you sure the exit is ahead?' (fired via notePlayerStruggle on death + the base wall-clock no-progress fallback) nudges suspicion WITHOUT spoiling that the exit is behind you. Intentional, guarded, and de-spoiled for VoiceOver (real door kept out of a11y order until reveal, lines 411/600). By design.

findings.title: Code-quality nits: hardcoded "LEVEL 29" title and unexplained 3.8s reveal duration
findings.note: Both real but purely cosmetic: the title could derive from levelID.index, and the wait durations are bare literals. No player-facing impact. Lowest priority; bundle into a style pass if ever touching the file.

topActions: Stop counting hesitations once hasReachedFakeExit is true (one-line guard in updatePlaying ~L813) so the PLAYER ANALYSIS / TRUST readout reflects real pre-reveal behavior instead of ~2 phantom cutscene hesitations — restores the level's thematic gotcha at near-zero risk.
Optional polish: frame-rate-normalize the updateCamera lerp (multiply/exponentiate by deltaTime) so the long traversal pans consistently on 60Hz vs 120Hz devices.
Optional polish: drop one mid-course world-space '<<' waypoint (or briefly re-glow the real door) on the walk-back so the return trip has a confirming beat before the spawn door scrolls into view.

verdictLine: ship — 1 real polish fix (double victory path), 1 phone-only cosmetic (bugs float off rung edges), rest false-positives/already-fixed; no crash or softlock, reach provably safe both devices

findings.title: handleExit fires BOTH base victory effects and a bespoke victory sequence — duplicated confetti/haptic/audio + a stray 'LEVEL COMPLETE' marquee over the fake-out
findings.note: handleExit() calls succeedLevel() (BaseLevelScene:508→playVictoryEffects() adds confetti, pops 'LEVEL COMPLETE', fires victory haptic/audio/flash/slow-mo) and THEN playVictorySequence() which independently adds its own confetti + HapticManager.victory + AudioManager.playVictory + flash + slowMotion. Effects double up and the generic base 'LEVEL COMPLETE' popText competes on-screen with the bespoke 'Y O U W I N' fake-out. Not a softlock: the two slowMotion calls share withKey:'slowMotion' so the second replaces the first (speed cleanly restores to 1.0 after 1.0s), and updatePlaying halts once state is .succeeded. Kimi was right about the root cause. Recommend: drop the redundant juice from playVictorySequence (or skip popText/confetti in playVictoryEffects for this level) so only the bespoke finale plays.

findings.title: Phone bugs scurry off the credit rung edges into mid-air (wrong column parity + offset magnitude + scurry range)
findings.note: Grounded the math: rung i_credit sits at x = w/2 ∓30 (zigzagOffset 30). createBugs maps bug i→platformIndex=(i*2)%10+1 then sets bug xOffset=±60 by platformIndex parity — which is the OPPOSITE parity to the rung's own side AND 2x the magnitude (60 vs 30), then adds scurryRange 50. Result e.g. platformIndex=1 (rung i_credit=0 at w/2−30, half-width 55 → right edge w/2+25) gets a bug centered at w/2+60, already 35pt past the edge, scurrying to w/2+110. Bugs visibly hover beside the boxes. Phone-only: the iPad placeBug() anchors bugs on platformCenter.x with ±40 scurry on 115-wide rungs and stays on-platform. Purely visual (bodies are non-dynamic, never fall) and the ladder is near-centered so it rarely intrudes on the jump arc — hence cosmetic, not fairness. Recommend: bug x = actual rung center, clamp scurry to rungHalfWidth − bugHalfWidth.

findings.title: Fourth-wall signs absent on the iPad path
findings.note: DeepSeek claimed buildComposedIPadLevel never adds the signs because 'the signs array is never initialized.' Current code explicitly builds and addChild's both 'YOU'RE STANDING ON THE PEOPLE WHO MADE ME.' and 'SAY THANK YOU.' on the iPad path (lines 405-421), positioned just above the TEACH platform. There is no 'signs array' — labels are created inline. False positive against current source.

findings.title: hintText() is static and ignores struggleCount — no per-death escalation
findings.note: This misreads the architecture. The base shows AT MOST ONE difficulty hint (showDifficultyHintIfNeeded is gated by hintShown, set true on first show). Every sibling level returns a single string from hintText(); per-death-escalating text is simply not how this engine works. handleDeath() correctly wires notePlayerStruggle() (the round-1 gap that was the real fix), which escalates via the struggle-count (2 deaths / 8s) and no-progress (~22s) paths. The one-line non-spoiler hint is intentional for a fake-out finale. Not a defect.

findings.title: centers.last! force-unwrap in composed iPad finale
findings.note: The beats loop runs `for tier in 0...topTier` with topTier = tierCount−1 and tierCount = max(8, …) ≥ 8, so centers always has ≥8 elements before centers.last! / centers[0] are touched. Cannot crash. Codex flagged it as 'probably safe but unnecessary' — confirmed safe; cosmetic style nit at most, no action needed.

findings.title: createBug scurry uses hardcoded duration/range that breaks on resize/dynamic width
findings.note: Gemini's stated mechanism (resize / dynamic platform width changes) doesn't apply: platform widths are fixed literals at build time and the level doesn't re-layout on resize. The real, narrower instance of 'bug walks off edge' is the phone createBugs parity/offset bug captured separately above (cosmetic). The generalized 'on resize' framing is a false positive.

findings.title: Completion state vs narrative position can diverge if app is killed mid victory-sequence
findings.note: By design and harmless. succeedLevel() records completion immediately; the ~20s victory fake-out then runs and returnToBoot() loads L31. If killed mid-sequence, L30 is marked complete (correct — the player DID finish it) and the deliberate 'glitched_reached_credits' flag (not the true game-complete flag, owned by L33) is set in showFinalScreen. On relaunch the player resumes at the next uncompleted level. No state corruption, no softlock. Codex's suggestion to shorten the handoff is taste, not a bug.

findings.title: VoiceOver focuses bug/exit at stale screen locations (isAccessibilityElement set, no accessibilityFrame)
findings.note: This is the documented intentional choice, not an oversight. The code comments explicitly state no accessibilityFrame is set BECAUSE these nodes scroll with the camera, so a fixed screen-space frame would be stale — they rely on label + staticText traits. Per the prompt's guidance, a deliberately-commented intentional decision is not a bug. At most a minor a11y-polish opportunity (live frame updates), not a defect.

findings.title: t=0 instruction-panel / title / spawn overlap (panel on finale tier & exit door on iPad; title-glyph & pause-column clip on iPhone; Bit spawning over TEACH text)
findings.note: Round-1 (Claude) headline against an older build. Current source carries an extensive HUD-overlap rework: title is camera-anchored at topSafeY−134 in the top-LEFT column (x[80,~210]) clear of the pause column, with documented iPhone-390/iPad-1024 screen-band math; the panel is camera-anchored at topSafeY−189 in a reserved-clear band (240-wide, below pause and title) — it no longer rides the centered credit ladder or the finale/exit on either device; setupBit spawns 40pt above the TEACH platform (iPad floor raised to playableGroundY) with a documented SPAWN-OVERLAP FIX so Bit no longer materializes behind the title or on the credit text. Verified against current line refs. Already addressed.

findings.title: Jump reach / completability
findings.note: Consensus is correct and confirmed against current code: phone rise fixed 76 < 85 cap with near-centered ±30 zigzag (mostly vertical hops); iPad derives tierCount=max(8, ceil(band/85)+1) so band/(N−1) ≤ 85 (each verticalTier step additionally clamped to maxJumpableRise=85 by the helper), and halfSpread=max(60,min(w/2−130,120)) ≤120 < 130 gap budget with single-slot zig steps. nextCredit() clamps to credits.count−1 so a large tierCount cannot index past the 10-credit array. No unjumpable gap, no crash. Not a defect — recorded only to close the panel's most-raised topic.

topActions: Remove the duplicated victory juice: in handleExit() let succeedLevel() own the win OR strip the redundant confetti/haptic/audio/flash/slowMotion + suppress the generic 'LEVEL COMPLETE' marquee so only the bespoke 'Y O U W I N' fake-out plays (Level30_CreditsFinale.swift:937/695).

Fix phone bug placement so hazards sit on their rungs: set bug x to the actual rung center (w/2 ∓zigzagOffset for the matching credit index) and clamp scurryRange to rungHalfWidth − bugHalfWidth (Level30_CreditsFinale.swift:492) — phone-only cosmetic, iPad placeBug already correct.

verdictLine: ship — 0 real fixes worth doing; 1 minor polish nicety optional. 6 false-positives, 2 already-handled-by-base, geometry/completability verified clean on both form factors.

findings.title: Hint ladder is all-or-nothing / no idle nudge before full reveal
findings.note: Two escalation paths already exist: struggle-count fires hintText() after 2 deaths + 8s, AND a base wall-clock no-progress safety-net (BaseLevelScene 714-718) fires after ~22s of no forward progress even with zero deaths. The 'no nudge between base timer and full reveal' is a design-taste critique, not a gap — a stuck player is never stranded. Multi-tier mid-nudges would be a nice-to-have, not load-bearing. Optional polish only.

findings.title: resetProgressTimer() used instead of notePlayerProgress() on flashlight-on / checkpoint
findings.note: resetProgressTimer() literally calls notePlayerProgress() (BaseLevelScene 731-733) — they are identical, both reset noProgressTimer, stamp lastProgressAt, zero struggleCount, and clear hintShown. The proposed change is a no-op rename. Codex was wrong that they behave differently.

findings.title: Light cone stutters: currentPitch only updated on discrete .flashlightAngleChanged events
findings.note: FlashlightManager posts .flashlightAngleChanged at 30Hz from CoreMotion deviceMotionUpdates (interval 1/30), NOT on orientation boundaries. currentPitch updates every frame, and updateLightCone lerps it (lerpSpeed 8). Gemini's premise is wrong; polling from DeviceManagerCoordinator in updatePlaying would be redundant double-reading the same sensor.

findings.title: caveCreatures retain cycle when a creature flees
findings.note: No cycle. The flee SKAction ends in .removeFromParent(); the next updateCaveCreatures pass drops it via removeAll{ $0.parent == nil } (line 1268). The blink closure captures the eye nodes (children of creature), not self, and ARC frees the whole subtree once it leaves both the scene graph and the transient array. The array holding a strong ref for ~1 frame is intentional bookkeeping, not a leak.

findings.title: instructionPanel force-unwrapped at gameCamera.addChild(instructionPanel!)
findings.note: instructionPanel is assigned a fresh SKNode() on line 1319, four lines above the unwrap. It cannot be nil at 1324. Cosmetic style nit at most; no crash path.

findings.title: isWideCanvas hardcoded 700pt falls back to iPhone layout in iPad Split View
findings.note: Intentional and matches the project-wide convention (L29 isWideCanvas, BaseLevelScene playableGroundY guards on size.height>1000). A narrow Split View slice genuinely should get the compact flat-cave layout rather than a stretched vertical climb; the flat layout is fully completable. Not a bug.

findings.title: tierNear strict < tie-break snaps stalactite x=2630 to the lower tier
findings.note: Real: x=2630 is exactly 90pt from both beat 12 (lower tier) and beat 13 (higher), and best only updates on d<bestD so the lower tier wins. But it does NOT affect fairness: gapFromFloor 175 puts the tip at tierY+195, ~104pt above a +91 jump apex from the lower tier, and the hazard sits over the gap the player passes UNDER. x=2050/2250/2440 are also equidistant pairs with the same generous clearance. Cosmetic authoring imprecision; fix by using <= only if you want the visual to hang from the tier you climb toward.

findings.title: iPad walk-by-one backfill can produce a 2-tier first jump at count=16
findings.note: The defensive backfill loop (497-503) stops at i>=1, so it can raise tiers[1] to tiers[2]-1 without re-checking tiers[0] (pinned to 0), yielding a floor->tier-2 opening jump. Reproduces ONLY at ipadTierCount=16, which needs band>=1275pt (canvas height ~1515pt+). Realistic iPads top out at count=14 (12.9in, band ~1082) where the rule is clean (verified: counts 10/11/12/14 produce zero bad rises and a reachable finale). Unreachable on shipping hardware, so harmless today; worth a one-line guard (also clamp tiers[1]<=1) only as future-proofing if larger canvases appear.

verdictLine: ship — 0 crash/softlock survivors; the panel's headline "stuck-alive player gets no help" is already-fixed by the base-class 22s no-progress fallback + explicit hintText; 1 polish survivor (graduated nudge), plus 1 new minor polish edge (two fingers on one pad); kimi's debug-gate-the-fallback and codex's resetProgressTimer findings are false-positives

findings.title: Stuck-but-alive player never gets escalating help (death-gated hints, no hazards in level)
findings.note: The panel is right that this level has NO hazards (only the player|hazard branch exists at L1274; nothing ever emits PhysicsCategory.hazard), so handleDeath/notePlayerStruggle realistically never fire and the death-gated escalation is dead here. BUT the base class already solves this: update() runs a wall-clock no-progress fallback (lastProgressAt seeded at startPlay L490; fires showDifficultyHintIfNeeded after ~22s of no forward progress, independent of death, L714-718). That displays hintText(), which on L32 is the FULL explicit instruction ('Hold every glowing contact pad in a group... two/three/four... then walk through'), also spoken via VoiceOver. So a confused-but-alive player IS rescued automatically. No action required for correctness.

findings.title: Single exhaustive hint instead of a graduated 2-step nudge
findings.note: The only genuine survivor: the safety-net fires one fully-spoiling hint rather than escalating ('try holding two glowing pads at once' -> 'keep them held while you walk Bit through'). Player is never stranded (gets full answer in ~22s), so this is taste/polish, not a fairness gap. Optional: override difficultyHintDidShow or add a soft first-nudge whisper before the full reveal. Low priority.

findings.title: Multi-touch fallback (handleGameInput .multiTouch) can open every gate without touching a plate
findings.note: Kimi recommends restricting this to simulator/debug. WRONG — it is a deliberate ACCESSIBILITY affordance. The .multiTouch(count:4) event is posted ONLY by accessibilityButton(for: .multiTouchPressure), which renders solely when accessibility.needsFallbackUI(for:) is true (a player who physically cannot perform a 4-finger simultaneous hold). activateNextFallbackGroup opening one group per tap is the intended bypass for that user. Debug-gating it would remove an accessibility path. Do not change.

findings.title: Opening a gate calls resetProgressTimer() instead of notePlayerProgress(), so hint state may not reset cleanly
findings.note: Factually wrong: resetProgressTimer() { notePlayerProgress() } — they are the SAME call (no L32 override). handleGroupActivated calls resetProgressTimer() which resets noProgressTimer, lastProgressAt, struggleCount, and hintShown exactly as notePlayerProgress would. Nothing to fix.

findings.title: Stuck plate left permanently active if touchesCancelled is dropped (Control Center / app-switch gesture)
findings.note: Two layers make this benign: (1) SpriteKit reliably delivers touchesCancelled for Control Center/app-switch system gestures, so the orphan rarely occurs. (2) Even if a plate stuck ACTIVE, it only ever HELPS — plate activation opens gates, and a completed section latches its gate open (handleGroupDeactivated returns early once sectionXComplete). There is no path where an extra-active plate blocks progress, so this is not a softlock. deepseek's 're-began touch with different pointer leaves old plate stuck' is also wrong: touchesEnded always fires for the original UITouch and removes it from trackedTouches before any re-begin. Active-validation pass in updatePlaying is unnecessary.

findings.title: movementTouch never reassigns -> player runs out of digits for the 4-plate finale
findings.note: No finger-starvation: gates LATCH open. handleGroupActivated sets sectionXComplete=true and handleGroupDeactivated returns early once complete, so the gate stays open after the group fires ONCE. The player holds the 4 plates momentarily -> gate opens permanently -> releases ALL fingers -> walks Bit through with a single touch. The hintText slightly OVER-states difficulty ('keep holding... while you walk through'), but the code is more forgiving than the hint. Plates are camera-children (HUD), fixed in screen space, so reach is independent of Bit's position. Completable on every device. movementTouch also reclaims after touchesEnded sets it nil.

findings.title: isWideCanvas magic-number threshold misclassifies 11-inch iPad in landscape -> drops to phone layout
findings.note: size.height>1000 is the project-wide iPad-portrait convention, shared by BaseLevelScene helpers (L38/73/82/101/115) and many levels (L1/L10/L12). The game runs portrait; iPad-landscape is not the targeted runtime for these layouts. Falling to the phone layout still yields a fully completable flat-ground walk. Not a L32-specific defect; changing it unilaterally would diverge from the whole codebase. At most a cosmetic codebase-wide note.

findings.title: iPad hand-composed climb is purely decorative / invites climbing toward high plates
findings.note: This is an explicit, documented design choice, not a bug. The mechanic is screen-space (hold a whole plate group while the gate stays visible), so a scrolling camera is intentionally omitted; gates 1/2/3 and the exit stay on the continuous ground precisely so the multi-touch gating can NEVER be climb-bypassed, and a continuous safety ground means a missed jump just drops Bit to walk-and-retry (no pit death). Per-tier rise clamped to maxJumpableRise (L419) and X-advance capped 96pt (L545) keep the climb within the <=85 rise / <=130 gap budget even though it is never required. 'Decorative climb' is a polish/taste critique the authors already weighed and rejected for sound reachability reasons. No completability or fairness impact.

findings.title: Two fingers on the same pad -> one lifting deactivates the pad while the other still holds it
findings.note: trackedTouches maps each touch to a plate index with no per-plate refcount. If two touches both land within 38pt of the same pad, both map to index i; when the FIRST lifts, deactivatePlate(at:i) flips isActive=false even though the second finger is still on it, and evaluatePlateGroups may visually close a not-yet-latched gate. Self-inflicted (requires deliberately stacking two fingers on one ~28pt pad) and fully recoverable (re-press), and completed gates latch open so it never softlocks. Minor polish only; a small per-index hold count would harden it if touched later. Not worth a dedicated fix.

findings.title: Plate-vs-jumpable-rung visual crowding on iPad (rungs overlap plate rings)
findings.note: On iPad the finale plate HUD (camera-children at zPosition 499-501) can visually overlap the composed climb rungs (scene-space zPosition 2) in places, which slightly muddies which dots are tappable HUD vs scenery. Purely cosmetic — plates are camera children and remain the touch targets regardless. Low priority readability nit only.

topActions: (Optional, polish) Add one graduated first-nudge before the full hintText so the no-progress safety net escalates ('try holding two glowing pads at once' -> then the full instruction) instead of firing the complete spoiler in one shot. The player is never stranded today, so this is taste, not a fix.

verdictLine: ship — flat finale is completability-bulletproof; 0 crash/softlock, 1 real cosmetic iPad-framing void + 1 rare cosmetic double-unlock race, the rest already-fixed or false-positive (hint hook, super.touchesBegan, dead review path, fragment physics all stale/intentional)

findings.title: handleDeath does not call notePlayerStruggle / stuck player gets no escalating help
findings.note: Stale against an older build. Current handleDeath() explicitly calls notePlayerStruggle() (line 1411, '// PROGRESSIVE HINT' comment), and the base class now has a wall-clock no-progress safety net (BaseLevelScene 714-718) that escalates a hint after ~22s on EVERY level regardless. The level also calls resetProgressTimer() at both gates and the review trigger. No action.

findings.title: touchesBegan never calls super, silently suppressing pause-button handling
findings.note: The pause button is a SwiftUI Button (LevelHeaderHUD) that calls GameState.shared.togglePause(); it is not routed through SKScene.touchesBegan. BaseLevelScene does not override touchesBegan at all, so there is no super behavior to suppress. Omitting super is correct here. No action.

findings.title: No real App Store review is requested; VALIDATE ME / .appReview path is dead
findings.note: Two parts. (1) The path is NOT dead: .appReviewReturned is posted by an accessibility 'star.fill / Return from the App Store review' button in GameRootView (298-299) and consumed by handleGameInput to unlock the door; DeviceManagerCoordinator.configure(.appReview) registers that affordance and deactivateAll() cleans it up. (2) Using a fictional non-soliciting button instead of SKStoreReviewController is DELIBERATE and documented (header lines 4-7, comment 966-968: never block completion on a real review, never imitate Apple's review UI). 'Wire a real prompt' contradicts documented intent. No action.

findings.title: Padlock fragments have no physics body, so they fall through the floor instead of bouncing
findings.note: Intentional: the fragments are pure SKAction particles (radial move/fade/rotate/scale over 0.4-0.7s) layered with ParticleFactory sparks, not dynamic bodies — they're meant to read as a shatter burst, not rigid debris. Adding dynamic bodies would also require restitution tuning and risk them colliding with Bit. Not a defect; at most a taste preference. No action.

findings.title: isWideCanvas bound (height>1000 && width>=820) also matches iPad landscape, contradicting the 'portrait' comment
findings.note: iPad landscape height is ~768-834 (< 1000), so isWideCanvas is false in landscape and the decor branch never fires there. gameplayVerticalLift also independently guards size.height>1000 (line 38), so even if the branch were entered, lift would be 0. The bound correctly selects only tall portrait canvases. No action.

findings.title: 10s fallback (reviewUnlockFallback) runs even after the button is tapped → double unlock
findings.note: As described, this is wrong: requestAppReview() calls removeAction(forKey:'reviewUnlockFallback') on line 1027, and handleGameInput(.appReviewReturned) removes it on 1314; unlockWithoutReview() also guards !exitUnlocked (1070). The tap path correctly cancels the fallback. (A different, much narrower race does exist — see the new finding below.) No action on this specific claim.

findings.title: Residual double-unlock race: button tap then .appReviewReturned within ~1.15s both reach unlockDoorFromOptionalReview
findings.note: exitUnlocked is only set in unlockExit ~1.15s after a tap (0.55s wait + 0.6s shatter wait). requestAppReview guards on !inLevelReviewButtonUsed, but handleGameInput(.appReviewReturned) guards only on !exitUnlocked, so if the in-scene VALIDATE ME tap fires first (sets inLevelReviewButtonUsed=true, removes fallback, calls unlockDoorFromOptionalReview) and the accessibility star posts .appReviewReturned in the same ~1.15s window, the event path re-enters unlockDoorFromOptionalReview → duplicate terminal lines, a second shatterPadlock burst, and a second unlockExit (double 'UNLOCKED' pop + double doorPulse key/physics enable). No crash, no softlock — unlockExit is effectively idempotent (re-adds keyed action, re-enables already-enabled physics). Requires hitting two mutually-exclusive affordances for the same action within ~1s, so extremely rare. Fix: add `guard !inLevelReviewButtonUsed` to the .appReviewReturned branch (line 1312) before it sets the flag, matching requestAppReview. One line.

findings.title: iPad framing leaves the entire lower ~40% of the canvas as empty dotted void below the ground line
findings.note: Confirmed math: gameplayVerticalLift(120,215) lifts groundY to ~0.42*canvas (≈534pt on a 1366 iPad), so the playfield sits at ~39% height and the band BELOW the ground line is empty (drawIPadUpperBandDecor only brackets ABOVE the ground, up to playableCeilingY). On a deliberately FLAT no-jump finale this is purely visual — zero impact on completability, gates, padlock-auto-break, or fairness; iPhone is untouched (lift==0). Lowest-priority survivor. Fix if polishing iPad: either lower the lift target (e.g. 0.30 so the ground sits in the lower third) or extend the decorative shaft pillars below the ground line. Cosmetic, not blocking.

findings.title: Generic 'LEVEL COMPLETE' marquee + confetti + slow-mo fires on the true finale before the game-complete cinematic
findings.note: handleExit() calls succeedLevel(), whose base playVictoryEffects() pops 'LEVEL COMPLETE', confetti, and a 0.3x slow-motion right as L33 starts its bespoke walk-into-door → blackout → 'SYSTEM OVERRIDE COMPLETE' cinematic. So the finale momentarily shows a generic per-level 'LEVEL COMPLETE' card (and the slow-mo slightly drags the door-walk) before the intended game-complete sequence. No functional problem (no UI auto-advances on .succeeded — GameRootView ignores it), purely tonal/polish. Optional: have L33 record completion + set state without the generic marquee, letting the cinematic stand alone.

topActions: Add `guard !inLevelReviewButtonUsed` to the .appReviewReturned branch in handleGameInput (Level33_AppReview.swift:1312) so the accessibility event can't re-enter unlockDoorFromOptionalReview during the ~1.15s before exitUnlocked flips — eliminates the only remaining double-shatter race (one line, cosmetic).

iPad-only polish: lower gameplayVerticalLift's target (or extend the decorative shaft pillars below the ground line) so the bottom ~40% of the iPad canvas isn't an empty dotted void on this flat finale — visual only, iPhone unaffected.
Optional tonal cleanup: suppress the generic 'LEVEL COMPLETE' marquee/confetti/slow-mo on L33 so the bespoke game-complete cinematic isn't preceded by a per-level completion card.
